abendego_diver.name: Abendego Diver
description: ## Abendego Diver
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/12›

Named for the vicious and unending storm in the southern Arcadian Ocean, Abendego divers have pushed their bodies to their limits, holding their breath to expand their lung capacity and swimming until ocean travel is second nature to them.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power note="An Abendego diver is not proficient with shields but is proficient with ‹eq-weapon/harpoon«s› and ‹eq-weapon/net«s›." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Ocean's Blessing]{icon=learn ability="An Abendego diver adds the following spells to his ranger spell list at the listed spell level. He still must select them as spells known, as normal." replace=Track s1="Hydraulic push~~wave shield" s2="Buoyancy~~elemental speech~ (water only)~~river whip" s3="Hydraulic torrent~~water breathing" s4="Control water~~ride the waves" }

::ab[Deep Diver (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost ability="An Abendego diver can ‹misc/hold his breath› for twice as long as usual (4 rounds &times& his Constitution score instead of the normal &times&2)." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Aquatic Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="An Abendego diver gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks while underwater." increment="p!These bonuses~3~5~4~2" replace="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Aquatic Adaptation (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="An Abendego diver gains a swim speed equal to his unmodified base speed. If he already has a swim speed, his swim speed increases by 10 feet." special="Because he has a swim speed, he gains the standard +8 bonus on Swim checks to perform a special action or evade a hazard, the ability to take 10 on a Swim check even if distracted or threatened, and the ability to use the run action while swimming if he swims in a straight line." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Shark Sense (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="An Abendego diver gains the ‹umr/scent› ability while underwater." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Killer of the Deep (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power flavor="An Abendego diver is peerless at detecting prey underwater and delivering killing blows." order=as ability="He can notice other creatures by scent in a 180-foot radius underwater and can detect blood in the water up to a mile away." standard="He can make a single attack against a *favored enemy* at his full attack bonus while underwater. If the attack hits, the target takes damage normally and must succeed at a Fortitude save or die. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + half the ranger's level + the ranger's Wisdom modifier. An Abendego diver can instead deal an additional amount of nonlethal damage equal to the creature's current hit points. A successful save negates this damage. An Abendego diver can use this ability once per day against each of his *favored enemy* types but not against the same creature more than once in a 24-hour period." replace="Master hunter"}

battle_scout.name: Battle Scout
description: ## Battle Scout
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/66›

Armies need clean water, safe routes, and intelligence about their enemies. Though regular outdoorsmen might suffice in many cases, some large forces look to battle scouts to keep the body of their troops safe and prepared for the terrain and whatever dangers that terrain hides.

::ab[Hunter's Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=down note="When a battle scout gains the *hunter's bond* ability, he must choose to bond with his companions. Battle scouts do not have animal companions." alter="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Advantageous Terrain (Ex)]{head l=5 icon=power ability="When a battle scout is within one of his *favored terrains,* he can spend up to 3 consecutive rounds studying an area of terrain to gain the benefits below. Each round of studying terrain is a @HLstandard_action. During the first round of studying terrain, the battle scout designates a 60-foot-radius-‹misc/burst› area from a single square within line of sight. Depending on how many rounds the battle scout spends studying the area, he or his allies gain the following benefits for 10 minutes per ranger level he possesses. (For example, if he spends 2 rounds, he or his allies gain the first two benefits.)" replace="2nd favored enemy"}
::ab[1st Round]{next icon=boost passive="Allies within line of sight and that can hear the battle scout gain a +2 bonus on initiative checks while within the *advantageous terrain* area."}

::ab[2nd Round]{next icon=boost passive="Allies within line of sight and that can hear the battle scout gain a +2 on Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks while within the *advantageous terrain* area."}

::ab[3rd Round]{next icon=boost ability="The battle scout's movement is not hampered by ‹rule/difficult terrain› while within the *advantageous terrain* area. Furthermore, the battle scout can take 10 on Climb and Swim checks and checks to jump while within the area, even when in immediate danger or distracted."}

::ab[Infiltration (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost ability="Once per day, a battle scout can choose a single terrain type that is not his *favored terrain.* For the next hour per ranger level, he treats that terrain as if it were one of his *favored terrains.*" replace="3rd favored enemy"}

::ab[Superior Tactics (Ex)]{l=15 icon=roll ability="Once per day, after a battle scout and his allies roll initiative, the battle scout can arrange his and his allies' initiative rolls any way he wishes. If he has already taken 3 rounds to scout out the terrain with his *advantageous terrain* ability and both he and his allies are within the effect area, they gain a +2 bonus to initiative checks. Using this ability is not an action." replace="4th favored enemy"}

::ab[Perfect Advantage (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="When using his *advantageous terrain* ability, a battle scout needs only to study the terrain as a @HLstandard_action to gain all of the benefits. Furthermore, the area of his *advantageous terrain* increases to a 1-mile-radius ‹misc/spread› from the single square within line of sight." replace="Master hunter"}

beast_master.name: Beast Master
description: ## Beast Master
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/124›

Some rangers, particularly those in primitive lands or who were raised by animals, have unusually strong bonds with animals. Unique among rangers, they can bond with multiple animals of any kind, creating a menagerie of wild yet loyal creatures, like a strange family. A beast master has the following class features.
::cskill{gain=acro~ea lose=kd~kg~prof~spc}

::ab[Animal Companion (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A beast master forms a close bond with an ‹sidekick/animal companion›.@FN1 This ability functions like the druid ‹ability/animal companion› ability except that the ranger's effective druid level is equal to his ranger level - 3. The ranger gains a +2 bonus on *wild empathy* and Handle Animal checks made regarding his animal companion. Unlike a normal ranger, a beast master's choice of animal companion is not limited to a subset of all possibile animal companion choices - he may choose freely among all animal companion choices, just as a druid can." note="The beast master may have more than one animal companion, but he must divide up his effective druid level between his companions to determine the abilities of each companion. For example, a beast master with an effective druid level of 4 can have one 4th-level companion, two 2nd-level companions, or one 1st-level and one 3rd-level companion." ability2="Each time a beast master's effective druid level increases, he must decide how to allocate the increase among his animal companions (including the option of adding a new 1st-level companion). Once an effective druid level is allocated to a particular companion, it cannot be redistributed while that companion is in the ranger's service (he must release a companion or wait until a companion dies to allocate its levels to another companion)." special="The *share spells* animal companion ability does not give the ranger the ability to cast a single spell so that it affects all of his animal companions." order=an2 replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Improved Empathic Link (Su)]{icon=power l=6 icon=power ability="The beast master gains@FN1-2 an empathic link with all of his animal companions. This functions like an empathic link with a ‹sidekick/familiar›, except the ranger can also see through a companion's eyes as a @HLswift_action, maintaining this connection as long as he likes (as long as the companion is within 1 mile) and ending it as a @HLfree_action." special="The ranger can only see through the eyes of one companion at a time, and is ‹misc/blinded› while maintaining this connection." replace="6th-level combat style feat"}

::ab[Strong Bond (Ex)]{l=12 icon=boost ability="The ranger strengthens his bond with his animal companions. The ranger's effective druid level for his animal companions is now equal to his ranger level; he may immediately allocate these additional levels to his companions as he sees fit." replace=Camouflage}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[The text does not specify when these abilities are granted. The listed levels are an educated guess, equal to the same level of the features they are replacing.]{from=1 multi=2}

blightwarden.name: Blightwarden
description: ## Blightwarden
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/17›
Some rangers feel so closely connected to their homelands that they stand as guardians against pervasive corruptions that pervert those lands beyond the point of recognition.

::ab[Hunt the Blighted (Ex)]{icon=down note="A blightwarden's first *favored enemy* is always a special type of creature: *blighted.* His bonuses from his first *favored enemy* apply to creatures with one or more of the following templates: ‹template/blighted fey›, ‹template/fungal creature›, ‹template/fungoid›, ‹template/Mana Wastes mutant›, ‹template/mutant›, ‹template/mutant goblin›, and ‹template/plagued beast›. Additionally, the bonuses apply against creatures with the ‹subtype/blight› subtype." special="Each time the blightwarden gains a new *favored enemy,* his additional +2 bonus always applies to this first *favored enemy.*" alter="Favored enemy"}
:::ab{icon=power title="Emulate Taint (Ex, Su, or Sp)" action="Standard Action" replace="Wild empathy"}

Once per day, a blightwarden can examine visible evidence of a creature that has been affected by a natural or supernatural blight. This might include visible tracks left by a creature with the *fungal creature* template, or a dead creature with the *blighted fey* template, or any similar evidence left by any creature as described in the *hunt the blighted* ability.

When the blightwarden does this, for the next 24 hours, he can use any one extraordinary, supernatural, or spell-like ability granted by the template (or the *blight* subtype) for a number of minutes equal to his ranger level. This duration need not be used all at once, but they must be spent in 1-minute increments. Saving throw DCs for this ability are calculated as if the blightwarden had the relevant template (for example, the DC of an ability from the *blighted fey* template would be equal to 10 + half the blightwarden's Hit Dice + his Constitution modifier). He cannot use abilities that would create a permanent effect beyond this ability's duration, and can never use more than one ability from a template or subtype in this way during a single 24-hour period.

For example, the blightwarden cannot use a blighted ooze's *cursed domain* ability to create a *domain of evil.* However, the blightwarden could gain a *blighted fey's thorn throw* ability for a number of minutes equal to his ranger level (in this case, the blightwarden would gain a thorn attack for that duration).

::ab[Blightwalker (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def passive="A blightwarden gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks when in areas afflicted by a natural or supernatural blight. In addition, the blightwarden adds this bonus to ability checks, saving throws, and skill checks to avoid natural and supernatural hazards in blighted areas. A blightwarden traveling through blighted areas leaves no trail and cannot be tracked (though he can leave a trail if he chooses)." choice="The blightwarden can choose to improve this bonus in place of improving a *favored terrain* bonus." order=pc special="Blighted areas include ‹rule/cursed land«s›, ‹rule/domains of evil›, regions negatively affected by a spell or ability that affects at least a 300-square-foot area, or regions where a specific ‹rule/affliction<s› is prevalent." alter="Favored terrain" replace="1st favored terrain"}

::ab[Resist Corruption (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A blightwarden gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against curses, diseases, mind-affecting effects, poisons, and transmutation effects." replace=Endurance}

bow_nomad.name: Bow Nomad
description: ## Bow Nomad
‹SOURCE People of the Stars/8›

Thanks to their four arms, kasathas can master a combat form native to their home world and rarely witnessed on Golarion - wielding two bows at once. This archetype is available to kasathas (and to other races with four or more arms at the GM's discretion).
::prereq{r=Kasatha}

::ab[Twin Bows (Ex)]{l=1 icon=ranged ability="A bow nomad can simultaneously wield a combination of two of any of the following ranged weapons: ‹eq-weapon/shortbow›, ‹eq-weapon/longbow›, and their ‹eq-weapon/composite« versions<_bow›." special="When a bow nomad makes a full attack with two bows, two-weapon penalties apply and can be offset with Two-Weapon Fighting feats. Since bows aren't light weapons, a bow nomad with ‹feat/Two-Weapon Fighting› takes a -4 penalty on attacks with each of her bows. Extra attacks from other sources, such as those granted by ‹feat/Manyshot› or ‹feat/Rapid Shot›, can be applied to only one of the wielded bows per round." replace="Wild empathy"}
::ab[Combat Style (Ex)]{l=2 icon=down choice="A bow nomad must select archery as her ‹ability/combat style<s›."}
::ab[Agile Maneuvers]{l=3 icon=power ability="A bow nomad gains ‹feat/Agile Maneuvers› as a bonus feat." replace=Endurance}

::ab[Trick Shot (Ex)]{head l=3 icon=ranged ability="A bow nomad can make a number of *trick shots* per day equal to 1/2 her ranger level + her Dexterity modifier. Unless otherwise noted, making a *trick shot* is a @HLstandard_action that provokes attacks of opportunity."}

::ab[Deflecting Arrow]{next icon=def-protect provokes=No immediate="A bow nomad can deflect a single ranged attack. This ability functions as the ‹feat/Deflect Arrows› feat, except the bow nomad need not have a hand free and can deflect an attack targeting any creature within 30 feet."}

::ab[Hampering Strike]{next prereq="8th-level ranger" icon=ranged ability="A bow nomad can attempt to ‹rule/disarm› or ‹rule/trip› an opponent within 30 feet in place of a ranged attack, with a -4 penalty on the combat maneuver check."}

::ab[Pinning Strike]{next prereq="13th-level ranger" icon=ranged ability="A bow nomad can attempt to pin a Large or smaller opponent's limb or clothing to the ground or a wall in place of an attack. If the bow nomad's attack roll exceeds her opponent's CMD, the opponent is pinned and cannot move from its current position. As a @HLmove_action, the opponent can attempt a Strength check to free itself (DC = 10 + 1/2 the bow nomad's ranger level + her Strength modifier)." special="Only creatures adjacent to a solid, anchored, penetrable surface can be affected by this ability."}

::ab[Exploit the Gap]{next prereq="18th-level ranger" icon=lower flavor="A bow nomad can exploit the natural weaknesses of her foes." ability="When a bow nomad makes a full attack with her twin bows, each arrow that deals damage in that attack also reduces the target's natural armor bonus (if any) by 1 (to a minimum of 0) for 1 round." replace="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Focused Fire (Ex)]{l=6 icon=down choice="The bow nomad must select the Manyshot feat as her 6th-level combat style feat." ability="In addition to the normal benefit of Manyshot, the bow nomad can specify the bow in her off hands as the source of the second arrow (‹misc/precision damage› and critical damage are still only added once). The bow nomad takes no two-weapon penalties when using this ability, but she cannot use it and Two-Weapon Fighting in the same round. The nomad can still choose to use Manyshot in the normal manner." order=ca special="At 11th and 16th level, the bow nomad can apply this ability to each of her iterative attacks." alter="6th-level combat style feat" replace="Camouflage~hide in plain sight"}

cinderwalker.name: Cinderwalker
description: ## Cinderwalker
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #95: Anvil of Fire/72›
Cinderwalkers traverse burning wastelands and restless mountains, thriving in lands most creatures avoid.

::ab[Born to the Fire (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def passive="A cinderwalker gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks when in volcanic or geothermally active areas. Such areas include calderas, hot springs, lava plains, and volcanically formed hills or mountains. In addition, the cinderwalker adds this bonus on any ability checks, saving throws, or skill checks to avoid geothermal or volcanic hazards. A cinderwalker traveling through volcanic or geothermally active areas leaves no trail and cannot be tracked (though he can leave a trail if he chooses)." special="The cinderwalker can choose to improve this bonus in place of improving a *favored terrain* bonus." replace="1st favored terrain~wild empathy"}

::ab[Inured (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A cinderwalker gains the benefits of ‹spell/endure elements› against hot environments." imp8="The cinderwalker gains fire resistance 10." imp12="The fire resistance increases to 20." imp16="The fire resistance increases to 30." imp20="He gains immunity to fire." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Cinderwalk (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost-def ability="A cinderwalker can move through any sort of solid volcanic terrain (such as ash, cooled lava, or volcanic rock) at his normal speed and without taking damage or suffering any other impairment." passive="The cinderwalker ignores fire damage from being near heat sources and touching heated surfaces (but not from immersion)." order=ap replace="Woodland stride"}

code_runner.name: Code Runner
description: ## Code Runner
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/40›

A code runner specializes in the subtle and speedy transfer of secret messages. He performs long runs between distant lands, sometimes spanning continents and often traversing difficult terrain. He trains his mind both to memorize long messages and to resist any attempts to steal his covert communiques.
::cskill{gain=kl~ling lose=ha~heal}

::ab[Mnemonic Genius (Ex)]{flavor="A code runner learns to memorize and accurately recall information, even if he does not speak or understand the language in which the information was presented." ability="The code runner can spend 1 hour and attempt a DC 15 Linguistics check to memorize a single passage of up to 150 words of information in a language he speaks. For every 5 by which he exceeds the DC, he can memorize 150 additional words. The DC is increased by 10 if the message is encoded or in a language he doesn't understand." icon=power special="A code runner can memorize one such message at any given time. If he fails to memorize a message, he can retry as many times as he wants, as long as he spends 1 hour each time. Once a message has been memorized, he can recall it with perfect accuracy until he memorizes a different message." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Resist Interrogation (Ex)]{icon=def l4="A code runner gains a bonus equal to half his ranger level on saving throws against divination effects." l11="If he succeeds at a saving throw against a divination effect that would grant its caster knowledge or information, the caster is not aware that the saving throw was successful, and the code runner can attempt a Bluff check opposed by the caster's Sense Motive check in order to send false information to the caster." replace="Hunter's bond"}

corpse_hunter.name: Corpse Hunter
description: ## Corpse Hunter
‹SOURCE Undead Slayer's Handbook/24›
Many rangers count the undead among their *favored enemies,* but some make a full-time job out of hunting down and destroying these walking corpses. A corpse hunter has the following class features.
::cskill{gain=kr lose=kna}

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn note="In addition to the default spells on the ranger spell list, a corpse hunter adds the following to his list of spells at the indicated spell levels." s1="Bless water~~detect undead~~hide from undead~~sanctify corpse" s2="Halt undead" s3="Speak with dead" s4="Searing light"}

::ab[Undead Exterminator]{l=1 icon=down note="A corpse hunter must select undead as his first and only *favored enemy,* granting him a +2 bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks when dealing with creatures of the undead type and a +2 bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against undead." increment="p!He does not gain an additional *favored enemy,* but his bonuses against undead~0~5~4~2"}

::ab[Disrupt Control (Su)]{l=5 icon=melee-zap flavor="A corpse hunter can infuse his attacks with divine power capable of harming an undead creature as well as its controller." standard="The corpse hunter can make a single melee attack against an undead creature, or a single ranged attack against an undead creature within 30 feet. If the attack is successful, he deals 1d4 points of ‹rule/Charisma damage› to the target undead creature in addition to normal damage. If the target undead creature is being controlled by another creature within 120 feet, the controller takes 1d4 points of Charisma damage as well; unlike the initial target, the controller may attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the corpse hunter's class level + the corpse hunter's Wisdom modifier) to halve the damage she would take from this attack." useInc=ranger~5 useF="5~1" imp10="The amount of Charisma damage dealt increases to 1d6." imp15="The amount of Charisma damage dealt increases to 1d8." imp20="The amount of Charisma damage dealt increases to 2d4."}

::ab[Graveyard Stride (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost passive="A corpse hunter may move easily through ‹rule/difficult terrain› in areas where corpses are interred, such as graveyards, catacombs, crypts, or necropolises. When in such an environment, the corpse hunter may move at his normal speed without taking damage or suffering any impairment, although any magical effects and enchantments that impair movement still affect him." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Incorporeal Armament (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost flavor="A corpse hunter can channel his will into any weapon he wields, making it effective against incorporeal undead." swift="A corpse hunter may imbue a single wielded weapon with the ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› special ability, causing it to deal damage normally against incorporeal creatures." useL=ranger~2 useNC order=wuf free="The corpse hunter can end this ability." replace="Swift tracker"}

dandy.name: Dandy
description: ## Dandy
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/41›

The antithesis of the gruff and wild woodsmen, a dandy takes effort to master the subtle etiquette of noble courts, the whispers of dark rumors, and the customs of the cultured world. Nonetheless, he is still a ranger at heart, and he hunts for rumors and gossip throughout the grand ballrooms of the wealthy and powerful just as most rangers hunt for enemies in the wilderness.
::cskill{gain=bluff~diplo~kl~kno~sm lose=ha~kd~kg~kna~sur}

::ab[Favored Nation (Ex)]{icon=power order=ca choice="Each time he would select a *favored enemy,* a dandy instead selects a country with whose courtly graces he is wholly versed and studied. He gains a +2 bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks against courtiers, officials, nobles, and gentry from that nation. Likewise, he gains a +2 bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against them." ability="A dandy can attempt the appropriate Knowledge checks untrained to identify such people from the selected country." special="A dandy gains additional favored nations, and can increase the bonuses of one of his current favored nations, in the same way he would with a normal ranger's *favored enemy.*" replace="Favored enemy"}
:::ab{title="Rumor Empathy (Ex)" action=Ability icon=power replace="Wild empathy"}

A dandy can alter the flow and spread of ‹rule/rumors› in his favor. This functions similarly to *wild empathy,* except that it takes 1 day (8 hours of work), instead of 1 minute, and it affects the general tenor of a rumor instead of a wild animal.

A successful *rumor empathy* check allows the dandy to increase or decrease the rumor's tone by one step. For example, he could make an indifferent rumor friendly and thus positive toward its target, or an unfriendly rumor hostile and thus even more negative toward its target than it was originally.

The difficulty of influencing a rumor depends on the size of the community in which it is spreading. The DC of the check is 15 in a village or smaller settlement. This DC increases by 5 and the number of days required to attempt the check increases by 2 for every ‹rule/settlement« size category<s_and_districts› larger than a village - to a maximum of DC 40 and 11 days to affect a rumor spreading through an entire metropolis.

If the rumor is only spreading through a smaller segment of the population, such as the elven district in a larger metropolis, use the population of that smaller segment to determine the DC and number of days of work required to use *rumor empathy.* The DC also increases by 10 if the rumor is about the dandy himself, since the dandy is obviously a biased source with respects to rumors about his own person.

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=down ability="A dandy gains *favored terrain (urban),* and though the ability continues to increase at the same levels, he doesn't add additional terrains." imp8="Whenever he is at a courtly function or party, his *favored terrain* bonuses increase by an additional 2." alter="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Hobnob (Ex)]{l=4 icon=down-boost note="A dandy must choose his allies as his *hunter's bond,* not an animal companion." ability="In addition to the usual *hunter's bond* ability, the dandy can spend 10 minutes at a particular event or party hobnobbing and introducing his allies to the right people in order to provide them with half the skill bonuses of his favored nation (though not the bonuses on attack and damage rolls) for the duration of the event." order=na alter="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Dandy Spells (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic flavor="The dandy gains a different sort of spellcasting than most rangers." note="A dandy uses his Charisma instead of his Wisdom to determine spells per day, DCs, and other factors related to spellcasting. He casts spells spontaneously from the ‹main/bard spell« list› (rather than the ranger spell list) with the same number of spells known and spells per day as a ‹class/medium› of his ranger level." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Party Crasher (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="The dandy is able to finagle his way into fancy parties, kings' balls, and other social events meant for the cream of society. To do so, he must attempt a DC 25 Diplomacy check, which takes 1d4 hours. If he succeeds, he finds himself an invitation, an invitee looking for a date, a staff member willing to look the other way, or some other way to enter the party. This does not allow him to break into restricted areas or to infiltrate strictly closed gatherings, such as secret meetings; it only allows him access to social events." replace="Woodland stride"}

darklands_sailor.name: Darklands Sailor
description: ## Darklands Sailor
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Darklands/27›

Those plying the lightless waters of the Darklands learn to use sound to augment their compromised vision, providing insight into the hazards lurking below the surface. A Darklands sailor has the following class features.

::ab[Skilled Pilot (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A Darklands sailor adds half his level (minimum 1) to Profession (sailor) and Survival checks to navigate or avoid natural hazards in subterranean waterways." replace=Track}

::ab[Keen Ear (Ex)]{icon=def-power-warning flavor="A Darklands sailor learns how to use sound to compensate for poor visibility when facing threats above and below the water." l3="He gains the ‹ability/uncanny dodge› rogue ability when underground and either swimming or aboard a boat." l8="He gains ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge› under these conditions, treating his Darklands sailor level as his effective rogue level for the purpose of denying an enemy's sneak attacks." l13="He gains ‹umr/blindsense› with a range of 30 feet against creatures touching water." l18="His blindsense range increases to 60 feet or equal to the range of his darkvision, whichever is greater." special="If the Darklands sailor becomes ‹misc/deafened›, he loses all benefits of this ability until his hearing is restored." replace="Favored terrain"}
::ab[Quick Swim (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost move="A Darklands sailor can swim at his full speed without penalty." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Subsonic Warning (Ex)]{l=8 icon=protect-boost swift="A Darklands sailor can provide the benefits of his *keen ear* ability to the subjects of his *hunter's bond.*" useMod=Wisdom useUnit=round useNC special="A deafened creature can't benefit from a Darklands sailor's subsonic warning." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Hidden Depths (Ex)]{icon=power l12="A Darklands sailor can use the Stealth skill to hide while underground or underwater, even if he doesn't have cover or concealment." l17="He can use the Stealth skill even while being observed when underground or underwater." replace="Camouflage~hide in plain sight"}

deep_walker.name: Deep Walker
description: ## Deep Walker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/66›

Some rangers devote their lives to the woods, becoming hunters, protectors, and wilderness guides among its diverse terrain types. The deep walker is instead a master of the strange terrain of the deep earth, exploring its caverns and caves, becoming a master of the dark corridors most surface dwellers fear to explore - and perhaps the reason they fear to explore them.

::ab[Deep Knowledge (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A deep walker gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (dungeoneering), Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks while underground (in caves and dungeons)." increment="The bonus~3~5~5~3" replace="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Rock Hopper (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost flavor="A deep walker instinctively grasps for the most stable surfaces and holds on tighter while moving over underground rock and stone." ability="The deep walker gains a +5 bonus on all Acrobatics and Climb checks made to traverse underground terrain. Furthermore, the deep walker ignores ‹rule/difficult terrain› created by underground terrain and subterranean flora." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Deep Walker Camouflage (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A deep walker can use the Stealth skill to hide in underground environments, even if the terrain does not grant cover or concealment." replace=Camouflage}
::ab[One with the Stone (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="A deep walker can use the Stealth skill while he is underground, even when being observed." replace="Hide in plain sight"}

divine_tracker.name: Divine Tracker
description: ## Divine Tracker
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/108›
Blessed by his deity, a divine tracker hunts down those he deems deserving of his retribution. His weapon is likely to find purchase in his *favored enemy.*
::prereq{other="A divine tracker's alignment must be within one step of his deity's, along either the law/chaos axis or the good/evil axis"}

::ab[Favored Weapon]{l=1 icon=power ability="A divine tracker becomes proficient with the favored weapon of his deity. If his deity's favored weapon is unarmed strike, he instead gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Blessings (Su)]{l=4 icon=power flavor="A divine tracker forms a close bond with his deity's ethos." choice="He selects two warpriest ‹ability/domains› from among the domains granted by his deity, and gains the minor ‹ability/blessings› of those domains. A divine tracker can select an alignment domain (Chaos, Evil, Good, or Law) only if his alignment matches that domain. A divine tracker uses his ranger level as his warpriest level to determine the effect of the blessing." special="If a divine tracker isn't devoted to a particular deity, he still selects two blessings to represent his spiritual inclinations and abilities, subject to GM approval. The restriction on alignment domains still applies." imp13="A divine tracker gains the major blessing from both of his domains." replace="Hunter's bond"}

dragon_hunter.name: Dragon Hunter
description: ## Dragon Hunter
‹SOURCE Dragonslayer's Handbook/22›
The quintessential dragonslayers, dragon hunters are experts at forging through the wilds in search of great wyrms. Dragon hunters have the following class features.
::cskill{gain=ka}

::ab[Predatory Deduction (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="When a dragon hunter successfully uses Survival to spot the trail of a creature of the dragon type, the hunter may attempt an immediate DC 15 Knowledge (arcana) check. On a success, the dragon hunter is able to deduce the species, age category, size, and color of the dragon." special="If the trail is less than 48 hours old, the dragon hunter gains a +2 circumstance bonus on the Knowledge check." replace=Track}

::ab[Wyrm Hatred (Ex)]{l=1 icon=info ability="A dragon hunter must select dragons as his *favored enemy,* granting him a +2 bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks against creatures of the dragon type. He also gains +2 bonuses on weapon attack and damage rolls against them." increment="p!These bonuses~0~5~4~2"}

::ab[Dragoncrafting (Ex)]{icon=power l5="Dragon hunters are experts not only at tracking and killing dragons, but also at using dragon bodies to craft unique equipment. A dragon hunter gains ‹feat/Dragoncrafting› as a bonus feat, even if she does not meet the prerequisites." replace="5th-level favored enemy"}

::ab[Undaunted (Ex)]{icon=def l10="A dragon hunter gains a +4 bonus on Will saves made to resist mind-affecting fear effects, Reflex saves made to avoid damage from breath weapons, Reflex saves made to avoid crush attacks, and Reflex saves made to avoid damage from tail sweep attacks." replace="10th-level favored enemy"}

::ab[Expert Dragoncrafter (Ex)]{icon=boost l15="A dragon hunter may take 10 on Heal and Craft skill checks to harvest dragoncraft materials and to create dragoncraft items. Additionally, the amount of time required to create a dragoncraft item is reduced by half." replace="15th-level favored enemy"}

::ab[Elemental Resilience (Su)]{l=20 icon=def ability="A dragon hunter gains energy ‹umr/resistance› 30 against a single energy type for 1 hour. The energy types he may choose are acid, cold, electricity, and fire." usage="Once per day" replace="20th-level favored enemy"}

drake_warden.name: Drake Warden
description: ## Drake Warden
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/24›

Some rangers specialize in dealing with rambunctious younger drakes, protecting them and teaching them to tolerate, and even trust, humanoid creatures. These drake wardens follow and pass along secret techniques for raising drakes effectively, and thanks to their methods, their drakes are both fiercely loyal and extremely useful for scouting and stealth missions.

::ab[Young Drake]{l=4 icon=power-down ability="A drake warden gains a ‹sidekick/drake« companion› instead of an animal companion, but his effective charge's level is equal to his ranger level - 3." repeatAt="The drake does not gain *drake powers* at this level.~14~18~~The drake does not increase in size.~12~16~20" replace="Hunter's bond~2nd favored enemy~swift tracker"}

dungeon_rover.name: Dungeon Rover
description: ## Dungeon Rover
‹SOURCE Dungeoneer's Handbook/22›
These rangers specialize in surviving hostile subterranean environs. They are well equipped to avoid traps, spot secret passages, and deal with inimical dungeon tenants.
::cskill{gain=ddev~ke lose=ride}

::ab[Stone Scouting (Ex)]{icon=def passive="A dungeon rover gains a +2 bonus on Perception checks to notice underground environmental hazards, including traps, potential cave-ins, and dangerous flora. A dungeon rover receives a check to notice these hazards whenever he passes within 10 feet of them, whether or not he is actively looking." replace=Track}

::ab[Vermin Affinity (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A dungeon rover gains the ability to improve the attitude of mindless subterranean creatures. This ability functions as the ‹ability/wild empathy› class feature, except a dungeon rover can influence any vermin (even those without Intelligence scores) instead of animals. Additionally, a dungeon rover can influence oozes with a -4 penalty on his vermin affinity check." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Dungeon Ally (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A dungeon rover may select a ‹companion/giant slug›, a ‹companion/giant centipede›, a ‹companion/goblin dog›, a ‹companion/giant scorpion›, or any poisonous snake (‹companion/reef snake›, ‹companion/riding python›, ‹companion/spitting cobra›, or ‹companion/viper›) as his ‹sidekick/animal companion›, in addition to the usual animal companion options available to rangers with the *hunter's bond* class feature." alter="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Subterranean Stride (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost passive="A dungeon rover is never slowed by ‹rule/difficult terrain› while underground. However, areas that are enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion still affect him." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Improved Stone Scouting (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost passive="A dungeon rover's bonus on Perception checks to notice underground environmental hazards increases to +4. Additionally, the rover receives a check to notice these irregularities whenever he passes within 15 feet of them." replace="Swift tracker"}

dusk_stalker.name: Dusk Stalker
description: ## Dusk Stalker
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/110›
Hunters and guides through the Shadow Plane, dusk stalkers are rangers that thrive in shadow. Adept at hunting in dusk, darkness, and twilight, these rangers excel at manipulating shadows.
::prereq{r=Fetchling}
::cskill{gain=kp lose=kna}

::ab[Shadow Guide]{icon=down-boost order=na note="When a dusk stalker gains the *favored terrain* ability, that ability is modified in the following ways." l=3 ability="A dusk stalker picks his primary terrain normally, but only gains a +1 bonus on those checks while on a plane other than the Shadow Plane, and gains a +3 bonus on those checks while on the Shadow Plane." special="Each time he chooses to add a bonus in a *favored terrain,* he gains a +1 bonus on those checks while on a plane other than the Shadow Plane, and gains a +3 bonus on those checks while on the Shadow Plane." alter="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Shadow Bond (Su)]{l=4 icon=def-lower flavor="A dusk stalker creates a mystical bond with shadows." passive="The shadows around a dusk stalker weave and swirl, confusing his enemies. When a dusk stalker is fighting in ‹rule/dim light› or darkness (magical or otherwise), he gains a +4 insight bonus on Acrobatics checks made to move through an enemy's threatened area or through its space." order=pau ability="The dusk stalker can manipulate shadows in a 5-foot square within 30 feet. That square must be in an area of dim light or darkness (magical or otherwise). Enemies with an Intelligence score within or adjacent to that 5-foot square take a -2 penalty to AC and on Reflex saving throws. The harassing shadows last for 1 round. This is a mind-affecting fear effect." useMod=Wisdom useUnit=time replace="Hunter's bond"}
::ab[Dark Sight (Su)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A dusk stalker gains the ‹umr/see in darkness› ability." replace=Camouflage}

elemental_envoy.name: Elemental Envoy
description: ## Elemental Envoy
‹SOURCE Disciple's Doctrine/5›
An elemental envoy travels the Elemental Planes. He has learned to protect himself from their many dangers, and he models his fighting style after their teachings.

::ab[Planar Combatant (Ex)]{l=2 head icon=power ability="An elemental envoy must choose the elemental combat style of the ‹arc-monk/elemental monk› archetype, choosing his combat style feats from those mentioned in the *Genie Style* ability of that archetype." alter="Combat style feats"}

::ab[Genie Style (Ex, Sp)]{icon=power flavor="Relevant text from the elemental monk archetype" l2="As a @HLswift_action an elemental monk can gain the benefits of one of the following style feats, based on one of the five types of genies. When she does so, she automatically enters that feat's stance: ‹feat/Djinni Style›, ‹feat/Efreeti Style›, ‹feat/Janni Style›, ‹feat/Marid Style›, or ‹feat/Shaitan Style›." l6="While the elemental monk is using a genie style feat, she also gains the benefits of a feat from the following list, as appropriate for that style's feat path: ‹feat/Djinni Spirit›, ‹feat/Efreeti Stance›, ‹feat/Janni Tempest›, ‹feat/Marid Spirit›, and ‹feat/Shaitan Skin›." l10="While the elemental monk is using a genie style feat, she also gains the benefits of a feat from the following list, as appropriate for that style's feat path: ‹feat/Djinni Spin›, ‹feat/Efreeti Touch›, ‹feat/Janni Rush›, ‹feat/Marid Coldsnap›, and ‹feat/Shaitan Earthblast›."}

::ab[Elemental Explorer (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-down note="Instead of selecting a *favored terrain,* an elemental envoy gains the typical *favored terrain* bonuses when traveling on the Plane of Air, Plane of Earth, Plane of Fire, and Plane of Water, except the elemental envoy gains a bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks instead of Knowledge (geography) checks." repeat="He does not select an additional *favored terrain,* but his *favored terrain* bonuses increase by 2.~8~5" alter="Favored terrain"}

falconer.name: Falconer
description: ## Falconer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/67›

Rangers have always enjoyed a special bond with a specific animal, but the falconer takes this bond to a deeper level. Falconers begin with their companion earlier than other rangers, and have the ability to teach their companions special tricks.

::ab[Feathered Companion (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A falconer earns the trust and companionship of a bird of prey. The bird can be of any type of large hunting or scavenging bird (even a vulture). This ability functions like the druid ‹ability/animal companion› ability (which is part of the *nature bond* class feature), but the falconer must take the ‹companion/bird› (or ‹companion/falcon›) ‹sidekick/animal companion›, and that companion has only half the normal hit points." special="The falconer cannot teach the bird of prey the *work* trick, but can teach it either the *roam* or *distract* trick for free. Whichever trick the falconer does not pick then can be picked as a trick later." head replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Roam (DC 15)]{next icon=power ability="The falconer can let his animal companion loose to roam and forage. He must let it roam for no more than a week. When the falconer lets it roam, it agrees to return to the place he let it loose within the time period he designates."}

::ab[Distract (DC 20; bird only)]{next icon=melee ability="The animal companion flutters wildly around any enemy it would normally attack with the *attack* trick. It makes an attack roll against that enemy. On a hit, the enemy is ‹misc/shaken› for 1 round."}

::ab[Hunter's Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=down-boost ability="A falconer must select an animal companion when he gains *hunter's bond.* He does not gain a new companion at 4th level; rather, his feathered companion gains full hit points."}

::ab[Swoop for the Kill (Ex)]{head l=6 icon=power ability="When outdoors, a falconer can direct his feathered companion to strike an enemy from high above. His feathered companion gains the following trick as a bonus trick." replace="6th-level combat style feat"}

::ab[Swooping Charge (DC 20; bird)]{next icon=melee ability="The falconer's bird companion flies up and then swoops down into a charge. To perform this trick, the companion must be able to fly, and it must be outdoors or someplace with enough room for the bird to fly great distances in (a grand cathedral may have enough room, but dungeons usually do not). It spends a @HLfull-round_action flying to a high vantage point, and on the next round makes a ‹rule/charge› attack upon an enemy the falconer designates. If that charge attack hits, the bird deals 2d4 points of damage instead of 1d4 with its bite and gains a &times&4 critical modifier when making the attack. If the bird hits, the target is ‹misc/staggered› for 1 round."}

flamewarden.name: Flamewarden
description: ## Flamewarden
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/72›
Emulating the blazing phoenix, flamewardens sweep through the world like a selective forest fire, burning away corruption, evil, and those who cling to decay.

::ab[Touch of Flame (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="A flamewarden can cause his weapons to burst into flame." standard="The ranger can grant a single weapon he holds the ‹magic-enh/flaming› special ability for 1 minute; while under this effect, the weapon counts as magic for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." useMod=Wisdom3 imp12="Weapons affected by this ability gain the ‹magic-enh/flaming burst› special ability instead." replace="Hunter's bond"}
::ab[Spells]{icon=learn note="A flamewarden can prepare spells from the ‹main/spells_>druid« list› that have the fire descriptor." alter=Spells}

::ab[Stoking the Embers (Sp)]{l=9 icon=aid flavor="A flamewarden can fan the last spark of a recently slain creature's life force back into a full flame." standard="Once per day, a flamewarden can touch the corpse of a creature that has died within 1 round to grant it the effects of ‹spell/breath of life›." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Burning Renewal (Su)]{l=12 icon=def order=ua2 flavor="A flamewarden can use the cleansing power of fire to remove afflictions from his body, mind, and soul." useMod=Wisdom3 ability="When the flamewarden takes 5 or more points of fire damage, he can remove one of the following conditions: fatigued, shaken, or sickened. Alternatively, he can remove 1 point of ‹rule/ability damage› or drain from an ability score of his choice." ability2="If the flamewarden takes 10 or more points of fire damage, he can remove one of the following conditions: exhausted, frightened, or nauseated. Alternatively, he can remove 1d6 points of ability damage or drain from an ability score of his choice." special="The ranger can use this ability only once each time he takes fire damage, regardless of how much damage it deals over the required amount. If the flamewarden has fire ‹umr/resistance› or ‹umr/immunity› to fire, fire damage he would have otherwise taken still counts toward activating *burning renewal.*" replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Phoenix Rising (Su)]{l=16 icon=aura-magic-aid passive="If a flamewarden dies, his body explodes in a conflagration in a 20-foot ‹misc/spread›. Each hostile creature in the area takes 10d6 points of fire damage unless it succeeds at a Reflex save (DC = 10 + half the ranger's level + his Constitution modifier) for half damage. The flames don't harm the flamewarden's allies, who instead are healed 5d6 points of damage, and any poisons they are currently suffering are neutralized as per ‹spell/neutralize poison›. The flamewarden's body is reduced to ashes by the explosion." imp20="The flamewarden gains immunity to fire, and once per week, the flamewarden rises from the ashes of his own destruction 1 round after dying. The flamewarden's body re-forms with all his equipment, and he returns to life with half his maximum hit points. Any ability scores that were 0 become 1, and nonmagical diseases and poisons are cured. A ‹spell/limited wish›, ‹spell/miracle›, or ‹spell/wish› spell cast before he rises from the ashes prevents him from doing so, as does the area of an ‹spell/unhallow› effect." replace="Improved evasion~master hunter"}

fortune_finder.name: Fortune-Finder
description: ## Fortune-Finder
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/29›

Vanaras often leave home to seek fortune and sate their curiosity regarding the mysterious lands that lie beyond their native territories. The most skilled of these thrillseekers are called ba-sadhaks, or fortune-finders. So successful have these canny explorers been at exploring Golarion at large that the secrets of their success are closely guarded, resulting in only extremely rare non-vanaran fortune-finders.
::ab[Hinterlander (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A fortune-finder adds 1/2 his level (minimum 1) on all Climb and Swim checks." replace=Track}

::ab[Adaptable Study (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="A fortune-finder can adapt and orient himself to new environments and new enemies." order=pm passive="He gains a +1 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks whenever he is in a terrain he hasn't selected as one of his *favored terrains.* He leaves no trail and can't be tracked in any such terrain (though he can leave a trail if he so chooses)." move="The fortune-finder can study an opponent he can see. He then gains a bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks attempted against that opponent, as well as on weapon attack and damage rolls against it. The bonus is equal to half the fortune-finder's highest *favored enemy* bonus." special="A fortune-finder can't attempt Knowledge checks untrained to identify an opponent he has studied with *adaptable study.* Bonuses from *adaptable study* don't stack with those from the *favored enemy* ability. The ranger can study only a single target at a time." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Trailblazer (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost ability="A fortune-finder can move through any sort of ‹rule/difficult terrain› at his normal speed. Terrain that is enchanted or magically manipulated to impede movement, however, still affects the fortune-finder." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Fast Study (Ex)]{icon=boost l11="A fortune-finder can study an opponent using *adaptable study* as a @HLswift or @HLmove_action. In addition to this, he can maintain bonuses from *adaptable study* against one additional opponent." imp19="He can study an opponent using *adaptable study* as an @HLimmediate, @HLswift, or @HLmove action and can maintain the bonuses from *adaptable study* against a total of three opponents." replace="Quarry~improved quarry"}

::ab[Master Explorer (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost flavor="A fortune-finder becomes a master explorer." ability="He can move through any sort of terrain that impedes movement at his full speed, even if it is enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion." order=ar fullround="He can study one opponent he can see and has already studied with *adaptable study,* increasing the benefits he gains from that ability to equal his full *favored enemy* bonus." replace="Master hunter"}

freebooter.name: Freebooter
description: ## Freebooter
‹SOURCE Pirates of the Inner Sea/23›

A freebooter is a natural leader, a pirate who works well with a variety of people and in a variety of roles. Her specialized combat tactics help organize and direct a crew, and freebooters' talents are in high demand. Most freebooters work as independent agents. A freebooter signs on with a crew when she feels the urge to travel, and often moves to a new ship when her contract ends.

::ab[Freebooter's Bane (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost move="The freebooter can indicate an enemy in combat and rally her allies to focus on that target. The freebooter and her allies gain a +1 bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against the target. This ability applies only to allies who can see or hear the freebooter and who are within 30 feet of the freebooter at the time she activates this ability." increment="The bonus~0~5" special="The *freebooter's bane* lasts until the target dies or the freebooter selects a new target." replace="Favored enemy"}

::ab[Freebooter's Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="A freebooter forms a bond with her crewmates. This bond allows her to spend a @HLmove_action to grant her allies extra combat prowess when they work as a team. All allies within 30 feet who can see or hear the freebooter gain an additional +2 bonus on attack rolls when ‹rule/flanking› with the freebooter or with another ally affected by this ability." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Fast Swimmer (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A freebooter may swim half her speed as a @HLmove_action or her normal speed as a @HLfull-round_action with a successful Swim check." order=ap passive="The freebooter gains a +2 bonus on Swim checks." replace="Woodland stride"}

galvanic_saboteur.name: Galvanic Saboteur
description: ## Galvanic Saboteur
‹SOURCE People of the River/25›

The transformation of the great warlord Kevoth-Kul into the hedonistic Black Sovereign a few decades ago was difficult for the people of Numeria to accept. Many Kellid locals blame Kevoth-Kul's change on the influence of the Technic League and view both the League and their mechanical minions with a mix of fear and distrust. Some Kellid rangers have since developed skills to defeat the League's robotic enforcers and devious arcanists.
::cskill{gain=ddev~ka lose=ha~kd}
::ab[Favored Enemy (Ex)]{l=1 icon=down choice="A galvanic saboteur must select construct creatures as his first *favored enemy.*" alter="Favored enemy"}

::ab[Reprogram (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A galvanic saboteur can improve the initial attitude of a mindless construct. This ability functions as ‹ability/wild empathy›, except only against mindless constructs. The ranger can use this ability against constructs with an Intelligence score, but he takes a -4 penalty on the check." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Lucky Dodge (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def flavor="A galvanic saboteur can dodge the precise attacks of even the most technologically advanced foes." passive="The ranger gains a luck bonus to AC against attacks that target his touch AC as long as his opponent's type matches that of his first *favored enemy.* This luck bonus is equal to 1/2 the ranger's *favored enemy* bonus against that creature type." incrementOrd="The ranger gains this luck bonus to AC against attacks that target his touch AC as long as his opponent's type matches~3~5" incrementDesc=his incrementEnd=" *favored enemy.*" special="A galvanic saboteur's animal companion (if he chose one for his *hunter's bond*) also gains this luck bonus." replace="Favored terrain"}
:::ab{l=7 icon=power title="Technological Trapsmith (Ex)" action=Ability replace="Woodland stride"}

A galvanic saboteur can rig technological traps using the remains of destroyed constructs with the clockwork or robot subtype. To successfully rig such a trap, the ranger must spend 1 minute working on the destroyed construct and succeed at a Disable Device check (DC = 10 + the construct's HD). On a failure, the trap doesn't function and the construct's remains are ruined. On a success, the ranger creates a mechanical trap with the following statistics.

::trap[Rigged Construct Trap]{cr=Variable mechanical pdc=20 dddc=20 trigger="location (all spaces occupied by the construct's remains)" eff="blast of electricity (1d6 electricity damage/2 HD of construct); Reflex negates (DC = 10 + 1/2 the ranger's level + his Intelligence modifier); multiple targets (all targets within 5 ft. of construct's remains)"}

Other types of destroyed constructs can serve as suitable materials for a rigged construct trap (at the GM's discretion). Constructs destroyed by ‹spell/disintegrate› or similar effects that leave no intact remains cannot be used to create a rigged construct trap.

::ab[Static Strike (Ex)]{l=8 icon=melee flavor="The galvanic saboteur has gathered enough parts and scrap from his constructed enemies that he can infuse some of his attacks with a bit of their alien energy." standard="Once per day, the ranger can make a *static strike* as a melee attack at his full base attack bonus. If the attack hits, it deals an additional 1d6 points of electricity damage and the ranger can make an additional attack at his full base attack bonus against a foe that is adjacent to the previous foe and still within his reach, as if he had ‹feat/Cleave›, except he doesn't take a -2 penalty to his AC and the second attack deals an additional 2d6 points of electricity damage." imp12="The ranger can continue to make attacks against adjacent creatures, as if he had ‹feat/Great Cleave›, with each hit increasing the amount of electricity damage dealt to the next foe in line." imp14="The ranger can use this ability twice per day." imp20="The ranger can use this ability three times per day." replace="Swift tracker~camouflage"}

::ab[Sensor Evasion (Su)]{l=17 icon=def flavor="A galvanic saboteur has mastered the art of evading technological and magical observation." ability="He can use the Stealth skill against constructs and magical sensors (such as those created by ‹spell/arcane eye› or ‹spell/clairaudience/clairvoyance›) even while being observed." replace="Hide in plain sight"}

groom.name: Groom
description: ## Groom
‹SOURCE Dungeoneer's Handbook/21›
The groom attends to the needs of the party's mounts and beasts of burden during their adventures, and guards these animals while the party goes underground.

::ab[Hide the Horses (Ex)]{flavor="A groom finds the best place to stable and conceal a party's mounts while the group explores a dungeon." passive="A groom adds half her level (minimum 1) on Stealth skill checks for both herself and the mounts she's hiding while she is concealing a party's mounts." icon=boost replace=Track}

::ab[Horse Whisperer (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic ability="A groom can use ‹spell/speak with animals› at will, but can use this power only on horses, riding dogs, and other mounts of the animal type." replace=Endurance}

::ab[Scout the Area (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-power passive="A groom gains a +4 bonus on Knowledge (local) checks." ability="She always knows where to find the nearest expert or spellcaster who can provide a particular service, such as the nearest source of ‹spell/raise dead›." order=pa replace="1st favored terrain"}

guide.name: Guide
description: ## Guide
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/125›

Many rangers are loners, but some choose to use their familiarity with the land to guide others safely through the wilderness. The guide forgoes a *favored enemy* to focus on the task or foe at hand, and can pass his knowledge and luck on to his charges. A guide has the following class features.

::ab[Ranger's Focus (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost swift="The guide can focus on a single enemy within line of sight. That creature remains the ranger's focus until it is reduced to 0 or fewer hit points or surrenders, or until the ranger designates a new focus, whichever occurs first. The ranger gains a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls against the target of his focus." increment="This bonus~0~5~4~2" useInc=ranger~3~1 useF="1~1~3" replace="Favored enemy"}

::ab[Terrain Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-power flavor="The guide forms a bond with the land itself, enabling him to direct others in such terrain." passive="When in his *favored terrain,* the ranger grants all allies within line of sight and that can hear him a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks." order=pa ability="As long as they travel with him, the ranger's allies leave no trail and can't be tracked. The ranger can choose for the group to leave a trail, or even specific members of the group to leave a trail if he so desires." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Ranger's Luck (Ex)]{icon=roll l9="The guide can either reroll one of his attack rolls or force an enemy who just hit him with an attack to reroll the attack roll. The ranger must take the result of the second roll even if it is worse." useInc=ranger~5~9 useF="9~1~5" replace=Evasion}

::ab[Inspired Moment (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power-boost free="The guide can have an inspired moment once per day. The ranger gains the following benefits until the end of his next turn. His speed increases by 10 feet. He can take an extra @HLmove or @HLswift_action on his turn. He gains a +4 bonus to AC and on attack rolls, skill checks, or ability checks. Finally, he automatically confirms any critical threat he scores." imp19="He can use this ability twice a day." replace="Quarry~improved quarry"}

::ab[Improved Ranger's Luck (Ex)]{icon=roll-boost l16="The ranger's luck increases. He gains a +4 bonus on his rerolls made with the *ranger's luck* ability, or if he forces an enemy to reroll an attack, that enemy takes a -4 penalty on the roll. This bonus or penalty is also applied on any roll to confirm critical hits." replace="Improved evasion"}

guildbreaker.name: Guildbreaker
description: ## Guildbreaker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/41›

Some rangers aren't the type to put their steel and sweat behind fighting particular kinds of creatures. Instead, these shrewd warriors alter their focus and dedicate their efforts to dismantling enemy organizations. Guildbreakers stoke their enmity for their foes, and they train to track the movements of rival organizations, and even to infiltrate these groups to gather secret intelligence.
::cskill{gain=bluff~dis~kl lose=ha~heal~kg}

::ab[Favored Organization (Ex)]{icon=down-power ability="When a guildbreaker selects a *favored enemy,* he can opt to select a specific organization, such as a particular thieves' guild, in lieu of a specific creature type. The guildbreaker must select this option for his first *favored enemy,* but he can choose either option for his later *favored enemies.*" special="The guildbreaker's bonuses from *favored enemy* apply against official members of this organization regardless of their creature type, but not against the organization's allies, paid mercenaries, or forced labor. This ability only applies if the guildbreaker knows that the target is a member of the organization." alter="Favored enemy"}

::ab[Read the City (Ex)]{icon=power flavor="A guildbreaker spends most of his time in cities and large population centers. As a result, he gains an innate sense of comings and goings in the hustle around him." ability="When in an urban environment, a guildbreaker can use the Survival skill to gather information, but only about the movements of creatures and what sort of people (races, social classes, predominant professions, and the like) occupy the community. Unlike with the normal way to gather information, the guildbreaker studies the signs of the city instead of asking around, so it doesn't spread word about the nature of his questions to others." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Deep Cover (Ex, Su)]{icon=boost-def flavor="Guildbreakers may work to dismantle their enemies' power structures from within, and they know how to gather resources and the necessary trappings in order to masquerade as a member of those organizations." l=4 passive="A guildbreaker adds a circumstance bonus equal to twice his *favored organization* bonus to all Bluff and Disguise checks to appear to be a member of the organization (this does not apply on Disguise checks to change his appearance and replace a specific member, but rather to checks related to appear to be a general member of the organization)." specialP="The bonus on Bluff checks from *deep cover* stacks with the guildbreaker's overall bonus from *favored organization* if the person he is attempting to deceive is also a member of that organization.~~~A guildbreaker posing as a member of one of his *favored enemy* organizations automatically defeats truth-telling magic (as if under the effects of ‹spell/glibness›), but only for the purpose of claiming to be a member of the organization. Truth-telling magic can still expose him with a more indirect question, such as a member of a non-religious evil organization asking the guildbreaker for the identity of his patron deity." replace="Hunter's bond"}
::ab[Crowd Stride (Ex)]{icon=boost l7="A guildbreaker can slip through crowded streets with ease. His movement speed is not reduced when moving through a crowd." replace="Woodland stride"}

hooded_champion.name: Hooded Champion
description: ## Hooded Champion
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/108›

The hooded champion lives on the periphery of civilized lands, and is often at odds with the forces of law and order. He is frequently a hero of oppressed peoples, lurking in the woods near their homes and trying to right the injustices inflicted upon them by the wealthy and powerful.

::ab[Panache (Ex)]{l=1 head icon=power ability="The hooded champion gains the ‹class/swashbuckler«'s› panache class feature. He regains panache through critical hits and killing blows when using a bow of any kind, rather than when using a light or one-handed piercing melee weapon." special="If the hooded champion has this ability and the panache ability from another class or archetype, the panache points from the two sources do not stack, but the hooded champion regains panache in any way either class feature allows him to. For example, a character with a Charisma score of 16 who has both the hooded champion's and the swashbuckler's panache has a base of 3 panache points, and regains panache from critical hits and killing blows from both bows and light or one-handed piercing melee weapons." replace="Favored enemy at 1st level"}
:::ab{next flavor="Text from the swashbuckler ability" icon=power action=Ability title="Panache (Ex)"}

At the start of each day, you gain a number of panache points equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). Your panache goes up or down throughout the day, but usually cannot go higher than your Charisma modifier (minimum 1), though feats and magic items can affect this maximum. You spend panache to accomplish deeds, and regain panache in the following ways.

Each time you confirm a critical hit, you regain 1 panache point. Confirming a critical hit on a ‹misc/helpless› or unaware creature or a creature that has fewer Hit Dice than ‹misc/half› your character level doesn't restore panache.

When you reduce a creature to 0 or fewer hit points while in combat, you regain 1 panache point. Destroying an unattended object, reducing a helpless or unaware creature to 0 or fewer hit points, or reducing a creature that has fewer Hit Dice than half your character level to 0 or fewer hit points doesn't restore any panache.

::ab[Deeds]{head icon=power l1="The hooded champion gains the swashbuckler's ‹swashdeed/derring-do› and ‹swashdeed/dodging panache› deeds, as well as the *dead aim* deed, below." l3="The hooded champion gains the swashbuckler's ‹swashdeed/kip-up› deed, as well as the *hooded champion's initiative* deed below." special="For all deeds, treat the hooded champion's ranger level as his swashbuckler level."}

::ab[Dead Aim (Ex)]{next icon=zap l1="The hooded champion's can spend 1 panache point when making a single ranged attack (not a full attack) with a bow to make a ranged touch attack instead. The target must be in the bow's first range increment."}

::ab[Hooded Champion's Initiative (Ex)]{next icon=boost-power l3="While the hooded champion has at least 1 panache point, he gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks. In addition, if he has the ‹feat/Quick Draw› feat, his hands are free and unrestrained, and his weapon isn't hidden, he can draw a single bow as part of the initiative check."}

::ab[Combat Style]{icon=down-power l2="The hooded champion must select the archery ‹ability/combat style<s›." l9="The hooded champion gains the ‹swashdeed/swashbuckler's grace› and ‹swashdeed/evasive› deeds." l16="The hooded champion gains the ‹swashdeed/swashbuckler's edge› and ‹swashdeed/cheat death› deeds." replace="Wild empathy~endurance~evasion~improved evasion"}

horse_lord.name: Horse Lord
description: ## Horse Lord
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/125›

Rangers of the plains use horses or other riding beasts to hunt their lands, forging a near-mystical relationship with their mounts. Horse lords are unparalleled mounted combatants, the envy of even the most dedicated cavalier. Though called "horse lords" as a generic term, these rangers are not restricted to horses for their animal companions - any creature the ranger can ride is included in these abilities. A horse lord has the following class features.
::ab[Combat Style Feat (Ex)]{l=2 icon=down choice="A horse lord must choose the mounted ‹ability/combat style<s›."}

::ab[Mounted Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-boost order=ap ability="The horse lord forms a bond with an animal he can use as a mount, which becomes his ‹sidekick/animal companion›. A Medium ranger can select a ‹companion/camel› or a ‹companion/horse›. A small ranger can select a ‹companion/pony› or ‹companion/wolf›, but can also select a ‹companion/boar› or ‹companion/dog› if he is at least 7th level. This ability functions like the druid ‹ability/animal companion› ability except that the ranger's effective druid level is equal to his ranger level - 3." passive="The ranger gains a +2 bonus on Handle Animal and Ride checks with his animal companion mount." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Strong Bond (Ex)]{l=12 icon=boost flavor="The horse lord strengthens his bond with his mount." ability="The ranger's effective druid level for his mount is now equal to his ranger level." replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Spiritual Bond (Su)]{l=17 icon=boost-protect ability="The horse lord can grant his animal companion ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to his ranger level once per day. While these temporary hit points last, when his mount is within 30 feet of the him, he can choose to share the damage taken by his mount as if using ‹spell/shield other›." replace="Hide in plain sight"}

ilsurian_archer.name: Ilsurian Archer
description: ## Ilsurian Archer
‹SOURCE Weapon Master's Handbook/6›

Ilsurian archers maintain the strong tradition of archery first developed by soldiers in the service of Ilsur, who raised a small army with which he intended to overthrow the monarch of Korvosa after the fall of the Chelish Empire. Though Ilsur's Arena teaches only the basics of these techniques, a few dedicated students strive toward true mastery in each generation.
::ab[Bullseye Shot (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="An Ilsurian archer gains ‹feat/Bullseye Shot› as a bonus feat. He need not meet its prerequisites." replace="Track~wild empathy"}
::ab[Archery Style (Ex)]{l=2 icon=down choice="An Ilsurian archer must select the archery ‹ability/combat style<s›." alter="Combat style"}

::ab[Vicious Aim (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="An Ilsurian archer adds half his highest *favored enemy* bonus to all attack rolls and damage rolls for attacks made with ranged weapons. This does not stack with his normal *favored enemy* bonus when targeting a creature that qualifies as a *favored enemy.*" replace="Spellcasting ability"}

::ab[Iomedae's Influence (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="An Ilsurian archer gains ‹feat/Weapon Focus› (longsword) as a bonus feat, due to the influence the church of ‹faith/Iomedae› has over Ilsurian causes." replace="Swift tracking"}
::ab[Pinpoint Targeting (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="An Ilsurian ranger gains ‹feat/Pinpoint Targeting› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet its prerequisites." replace=Quarry}
::ab[Quarry (Ex)]{l=19 icon=power ability="An Ilsurian ranger gains the *quarry* class feature." replace="Improved quarry"}

infiltrator.name: Infiltrator
description: ## Infiltrator
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/125›

Some rangers study their *favored enemies* and learn their ways, applying this knowledge to their own abilities and using their foes' strengths against them. Infiltrators are willing to walk a mile in an enemy's shoes so as to learn eveything there is to know about their foes in order to more effectively hunt and kill them.

::ab[Adaptation (Ex)]{l=3 head icon=power-def flavor="An infiltrator learns how to copy the unusual abilities of his prey." useL=ranger~10 useUnit=minute useNC=10 ability="He chooses one type of creature he has selected as a *favored enemy,* such as &quot&aberrations.&quot& The ranger selects one ability or feat from the adaptation list for that type (see below). If the adaptation requires the ranger to make a more specific choice (such as what skill to use with ‹feat/Skill Focus›), this choice is permanent and cannot be changed." repeatAt="The ranger chooses another one of his *favored enemy* types and selects one adaptation from that type's list, as well as an additional adaptation from any one list of a creature type he's selected (including the one just chosen, if so desired).~8~13~15" special="The infiltrator can only use one adaptation at a time. The ranger can use the *camouflage* and *hide in plain sight* class features whenever he is using adaptations." replace="Favored terrain"}
::ab[Aberration]{next icon=power-def choice="‹umr/amphibious›, darkvision 60 ft., ‹feat/Iron Will›, natural armor +2."}
::ab[Animal]{next icon=power-def choice="climb 15 ft., darkvision 60 ft., ‹feat/Great Fortitude›, ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes›, low-light vision, natural armor (+2), swim 15 ft."}
::ab[Construct]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., Great Fortitude, Lightning Reflexes, low-light vision."}
::ab[Dragon]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., Great Fortitude, Iron Will, Lightning Reflexes, low-light vision, ‹feat/Lunge›."}
::ab[Fey]{next icon=def-power choice="Iron Will, Lightning Reflexes, low-light vision."}
::ab[Humanoid (aquatic)]{next icon=power-def choice="low-light vision, natural armor +2, darkvision, swim 15 ft."}
::ab[Humanoid (dwarf)]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., *‹race/d«efensive training<warf›, greed, hatred, hardy, slow and steady, stability, stonecunning, weapon familiarity.*"}

::ab[Humanoid (elf)]{next icon=power-def choice="*‹race/half_>elf› blood,* low-light vision, *‹race/elven› immunities, elven magic, keen senses,* Skill Focus (choose one skill), *weapon familiarity.*"}
::ab[Humanoid (giant)]{next icon=power-def choice="low-light vision, Lunge, natural armor +2, ‹umr/resist› energy 10 (choose cold, electricity, or fire), ‹feat/Throw Anything›."}
::ab[Humanoid (gnoll)]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency› (‹eq-weapon/dire flail›), natural armor +2."}
::ab[Humanoid (gnome)]{next icon=power-def choice="defensive training, *‹race/gnome› magic, hatred, illusion resistance, keen senses, low-light vision, obsessive, weapon familiarity.*"}
::ab[Humanoid (goblinoid)]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., goblin fast,@FN1 Skill Focus (Stealth), natural armor (+2)."}
::ab[Humanoid (halfling)]{next icon=power-def choice="*fearless, ‹race/halfling› luck, keen senses, sure-footed, weapon familiarity.*"}
::ab[Humanoid (human)]{next icon=power-def choice="Great Fortitude, Iron Will, Lightning Reflexes, Skill Focus (choose one skill)."}
::ab[Humanoid (orc)]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., ‹umr/ferocity›, *‹race/half_orc>«intimidating›, orc blood, weapon familiarity.*"}

::ab[Humanoid (reptilian)]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., *kobold fast* (as goblin fast@FN1-2), ‹monster/lizardfolk› *hold breath,* natural armor +2, Skill Focus (choose Acrobatics, Perception, or Stealth)."}
::ab[Magical Beast]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., Great Fortitude, low-light vision, natural armor +2."}
::ab[Monstrous Humanoid]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., Lightning Reflexes, low-light vision, natural armor +2."}
::ab[Ooze]{next icon=power-def choice="acid resistance 10, Great Fortitude, Iron Will, Skill Focus (choose Climb, Escape Artist, or Perception)."}
::ab[Outsider]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., energy resistance 5 (choose one type of energy from acid, cold, electricity, or fire), Great Fortitude, Iron Will, Lightning Reflexes."}
::ab[Plant]{next icon=power-def choice="low-light vision, Great Fortitude, Iron Will."}
::ab[Undead]{next icon=power-def choice="darkvision 60 ft., Great Fortitude, Iron Will, natural armor (+2). Skill Focus (Stealth)."}
::ab[Vermin]{next icon=power-def choice="climb 15 ft., darkvision 60 ft., Great Fortitude, Iron Will, swim 15 ft."}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN["Goblin fast" is not listed anywhere. It likely refers to a ‹monster/goblin«'s› base speed of 30 feet, which is +10 feet faster than most Small creatures and races. Consult your GM.]{from=1 multi=2}

jungle_lord.name: Jungle Lord
description: ## Jungle Lord
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Worldscape #4/25›

Whether raised from birth under the jungle canopy in a native tribe or settlement, marooned as a child and reared by animals, or simply an ordinary person lost in the trackless tangle and forced to thrive in the wilderness or die, jungle lords are tempered by their harsh and unforgiving environments into something more than ordinary men and women. Their ordeals and experiences combine the physical prowess gained as a result of life hunting dangerous beasts with the wisdom gained from surviving countless natural dangers to earn them the respect of the jungle's denizens, man and animal alike.
::cskill{gain=acro~ling lose=kd~spc}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down ability="A jungle lord is proficient with the ‹eq-weapon/dagger›, ‹eq-weapon/battleaxe›, ‹eq-weapon/blowgun›, ‹eq-weapon/bolas›, ‹eq-weapon/club›, ‹eq-weapon/greatclub›, ‹eq-weapon/handaxe›, ‹eq-weapon/kukri›, ‹eq-weapon/lasso›, ‹eq-weapon/longbow›, ‹eq-weapon/longspear›, ‹eq-weapon/net›, ‹eq-weapon/quarterstaff›, ‹eq-weapon/shortbow›, ‹eq-weapon/shortspear›, ‹eq-weapon/sling›, ‹eq-weapon/spear›, and ‹eq-weapon/whip›. Jungle Lords are not proficient with armor and shields." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}
::ab[Bonus Language]{icon=info note="A jungle lord's language options include Sylvan, the language of woodland creatures."}

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{icon=power swift="A jungle lord can take on the aspect of an animal, gaining a bonus or a special ability based on the type of animal emulated. This ability functions as the hunter's ‹ability/animal focus› class feature, though it applies only to the jungle lord and not an animal companion." useL=ranger useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="He can emulate only one animal at a time." replace="Favored enemy"}
::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=down choice="A jungle lord must select jungle as his first *favored terrain.*" alter="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Hunter's Bond (Ex)]{icon=power-boost ability="A jungle lord forms a close bond with an ‹sidekick/animal companion›.
