This ability functions like the druid ‹ability/animal companion› ability except that the jungle lord's effective druid level is equal to his ranger level - 3." passive="The jungle lord gains a +2 bonus on *wild empathy* and Handle Animal checks regarding his animal companion." order=apc choice="A jungle lord can choose his companion from the following list: ‹companion/ape›, ‹companion/axe beak›, ‹companion/baboon›, ‹companion/bear›, ‹companion/big cat› (lion, tiger), ‹companion/bird›, ‹companion/boar›, ‹companion/crocodile› (alligator), dinosaur (‹companion/deinonychus›, ‹companion/pteranodon›, ‹companion/velociraptor›), ‹companion/dog›, ‹companion/elephant›, ‹companion/giant chameleon›, ‹companion/giant gecko›, ‹companion/giant vulture›, ‹companion/giraffe›, ‹companion/hippopotamus›, ‹companion/moa›, ‹companion/monitor lizard›, ‹companion/rhinoceros›, ‹companion/small cat› (cheetah, leopard), snake (‹companion/constrictor›, ‹companion/viper›), or ‹companion/wolf›" special="The jungle lord can have up to four animal companions, but he must divide up his efiective druid level between his companions to determine the abilities of each companion. For example, a jungle lord with an effective druid level of 4 can have one 4th-level companion, two 2nd-level companions, or one lst-level and one 3rd-level companion. Each time a jungle lord's effective druid level increases, he must decide how to allocate the increase among his animal companions (including the option of adding a new 1st-level companion). Once an effective druid level is allocated to a particular companion, it cannot be redistributed while that companion is in the jungle lord's service (he must release a companion or wait until a companion dies to allocate its levels to another companion)." alter="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Hardened by Nature (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def flavor="The jungle lord's keen senses make him harder to hit and more able to withstand those blows that manage to sneak by his defenses." passive="When unarmored and unencumbered, the jungle lord can add his Wisdom bonus (if any) to his AC and his CMB." imp6="The jungle lord gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC." increment="The dodge bonus~6~3" special="He loses these bonuses when he wears any armor, when he carries a shield, or when he carries a medium or heavy load." replace="Spellcasting ability"}

::ab[Brachiation (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost useL=ranger useNC free="A jungle lord can climb with a climb speed equal to his land speed, and gains a bonus on Acrobatics checks equal to his ranger level." replace="Favored terrain gained at 5th level"}

::ab[Inspired Moment (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power-def free="The jungle lord can have an *inspired moment* once per day. The jungle lord gains the following benefits until the end of his next turn. His speed increases by 10 feet. He can take an extra @HLmove or @HLswift_action on his turn. He gains a +4 bonus to AC and on attack rolls, skill checks, or ability checks. Finally, he automatically confirms any critical threat he scores." imp19="He can use this ability one additional time per day." replace="Quarry~improved quarry"}

::ab[Strong Bond (Ex)]{l=12 icon=boost flavor="The jungle lord strengthens his bond with his animal companions." ability="The jungle lord's effective druid level for his animal companions is now equal to his ranger level; he can immediately allocate these additional levels to his companions as he sees fit." replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Victory Cry (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost-protect flavor="The jungle lord's signature battle cry rallies his animal friends to greater combat prowess and hardiness." useMod=Wisdom standard="The jungle lord can shout his victory cry. Each time he does so, his animal companions within 200 feet gain a +6 morale bonus on attack rolls and to AC, and all threatened critical hits made by his animal companions are automatically confirmed. Furthermore, following his victory cry, the jungle lord can apply a number of *animal aspects* equal to his Wisdom modifier when using animal focus, rather than the usual limit of one. These effects last for 1 minute." replace="Master hunter"}

lantern_lighter.name: Lantern Lighter
description: ## Lantern Lighter
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/113;Inner Sea Intrigue/42›

Lantern lighters were originally entrusted with secretly eliminating the drow and quarantining any knowledge of their existence, but in the new ‹rule/Lantern Bearer<s› era, the lantern lighters have refocused their efforts on helping to rescue those imprisoned by the drow, or even to help individual drow who seek to escape the cruelties of their society for a chance at redemption on the surface. Of course, escape from drow society is only the beginning of a drow's long and fraught path to redemption, and even the kindest or most repentant drow are likely to face prejudice from most of those who recognize her origins. In addition to their rescue efforts, lantern lighters often serve as sponsors for escapees and work to create pockets of civilization where redeemed drow can live in relative peace and safety by encouraging surface dwellers to cast aside the lurking shadows of prejudice and fear.

While the vast majority of lantern lighters are ‹race/elves› from Kyonin, a few non-elven members of the organization have proven themselves trustworthy and dedicated enough to join their ranks. As their efforts to spread the light to other regions beyond Kyonin grow, lantern lighters of other races are beginning to round out the order's numbers, particularly in areas and regions that suffer under oppressive regimes.
::ab[Daylight]{icon=learn ability="A lantern lighter treats ‹spell/daylight› as a 3rd-level ranger spell." alter=Spells}

::ab[Enhanced Vision (Ex)]{flavor="A lantern lighter hones his senses to better combat subterranean enemies." icon=power ability="He gains low-light vision. If the lantern lighter already has low-light vision, he can instead see three times as far as humans in areas of dim light." replace="Wild empathy"}
::ab[Poison Resistance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A lantern lighter gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against poison." replace=Endurance}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=down ability="A lantern lighter selects a *favored terrain* as normal." repeat="The skill bonus and initiative bonus granted in this *favored terrain* increase by 2.~8~5" alter="Favored terrain"}
::ab[Cavern Stride (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost ability="A lantern lighter's movement is unimpeded by nonmagical ‹rule/difficult terrain› underground and nonmagical webs." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Darkvision (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A lantern lighter's senses fully attune to the darkness, granting him darkvision to a distance of 60 feet. If the lantern lighter already has darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet." replace="8th-level favored terrain"}
::ab[Poison Immunity (Ex)]{l=12 icon=def passive="A lantern lighter gains immunity to poison." replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Stunning Light (Su)]{l=13 icon=lower flavor="A lantern lighter learns to increase the effectiveness of bright light on creatures with light blindness." ability="*Daylight* spells cast by the lantern lighter or other sources of bright light he wields cause creatures with ‹umr/light blindness› within the area of bright light to become ‹misc/stunned› for 1 round in addition to being blinded." replace="Favored terrain gained at 13th level"}
::ab[Camouflage (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="A lantern lighter gains *camouflage,* as the ranger class feature." replace="Hide in plain sight"}

::ab[Paralyzing Light (Su)]{l=18 icon=lower flavor="A lantern lighter can stop light-blind enemies in their tracks, often gaining a great edge in battle." ability="*Daylight* spells cast by the lantern lighter or sources of bright light wielded by him cause creatures with light blindness within the area of bright light to become ‹rule/paralyzed› for 1 round in addition to being blinded and stunned." replace="Favored terrain gained at 18th level"}

nirmathi_irregular.name: Nirmathi Irregular
description: ## Nirmathi Irregular
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Magic/37›

The forested country of Nirmathas is known for its rangers - skilled trackers, marksmen, and silent stalkers under the leafy canopy. Nirmathas has no formal military, for her people love their freedom and are reluctant to take orders from anyone, so her defense falls to individual scouts and small groups of allied commandos. Many of Nirmathas's rangers take the ‹arc-ranger/guide› or ‹arc-ranger/skirmisher› archetypes, but some focus on the magic of stealth. These irregular troops fight against the frequent invasions by Molthuni soldiers, striking quickly and melting into the green shadows as soon as their opponents rally themselves for a counterattack. A Nirmathi irregular has the following class features.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down ability="A Nirmathi irregular is proficient with all simple and martial weapons and with light armor." replace="Armor and weapon proficiency"}

::ab[Focused Enemy (Ex)]{l=1 alter="Favored enemy" icon=down ability="A Nirmathi irregular selects one *favored enemy* (usually &quot&humanoid &#91&human&#93&&quot&). He does not gain additional *favored enemies* at higher levels." increment="The *favored enemy* bonus~0~5~4~2" special="This otherwise acts as and replaces the standard *favored enemy* ability."}

::ab[Forest Ghost (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A Nirmathi irregular adds a bonus equal to 1/2 his level on all Perception and Survival skill checks he makes while located in forest terrain.@FN1" replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Focused Terrain]{l=3 alter="Favored terrain" icon=down ability="A Nirmathi irregular selects one *favored terrain* (usually &quot&forest&quot&). He does not gain additional *favored terrains* at higher levels." increment="The *favored terrain* bonus~3~5~4~2" special="This otherwise acts as and replaces the standard *favored enemy* ability."}

::ab[Spells]{l=4 icon=learn order=a2 ability="A Nirmathi irregular increases his number of spells per day by +1 for every spell level. This means the irregular gets at least 1 spell per day as soon as he gains access to a level of spells, even if the ranger's spell table lists 0 under spells per day." ability2="A Nirmathi irregular also learns to draw upon druidic magic, perhaps from association with the druids of Crystalhurst or a natural connection to the magic of the Fangwood. Each day when the ranger prepares spells, he may choose one ‹main/druid spell› and prepare it as if it were on the ranger spell list as a ranger spell of its druid spell level."}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[There is no listed minimum, so the Nirmathi irregular starts out with a +0 bonus at 1st level.]{from=1}

planar_scout.name: Planar Scout
description: ## Planar Scout
‹SOURCE Planar Adventures/17›
Planar scouts are rangers specializing in traversing the planes - and surviving their journeys.
::cskill{gain=kp lose=kd}

::ab[Planar Empathy (Ex)]{icon=down-power ability="A planar scout can use *wild empathy* only on animals with the ‹subtype/extraplanar› subtype. He can use this ability to influence outsiders with an Intelligence score of 1 or 2, but he takes a -4 penalty on the check." alter="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Planar Terrains (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A planar scout gains a planar terrain. *Planar terrains* function in all ways like a ranger's *favored terrain;* he must select a new plane each time he gains a *favored terrain.*" alter="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Planar Bond (Su)]{l=4 icon=power head ability="Rather than forge a bond with an animal or his allies, a planar scout forms a bond with a specific plane. The planar scout becomes infused with the essence of his plane of choice, taking on physical characteristics of denizens native to his chosen plane as well as the abilities listed below." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Aligned Plane]{icon=boost next ability="If the planar scout selects a plane with an alignment trait, he gains a bonus on attack and damage rolls against outsiders and creatures with the extraplanar subtype whose alignments oppose this alignment trait. @B-4m-ranger."}
::ab[Astral Plane]{icon=magic next move="Each day, the planar scout can teleport up to 5 feet per ranger level. This must be used in 5-foot increments." imp10="He can do so as a @HLswift_action."}

::ab[Ethereal Plane]{icon=boost next ability="The planar scout can damage incorporeal creatures with mundane weapons as if they were ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› weapons." imp10="He can inflict critical hits and deal ‹misc/precision damage› to incorporeal creatures."}
::ab[First World]{icon=aid next useMod=Wisdom swift="A planar scout can gain ‹umr/fast healing› 2 for a number of rounds equal to his ranger level."}

::ab[Plane of Air]{icon=power next swift="The planar scout can gain a fly speed (average) equal to his base speed for a number of minutes equal to his ranger level (minimum 1 minute). This duration need not be used consecutively."}
::ab[Plane of Earth]{icon=def next ability="The planar scout gains a bonus to his natural armor. @B-4m-ranger-x."}

::ab[Plane of Fire]{icon=def-boost next swift="The planar scout can wreathe his body in flames. This grants the planar scout fire resistance 5 and causes his melee attacks to each deal 1d4 additional points of fire damage." useL=ranger useNC incrementPlain="The scout's fire resistance~4~5~10~5" incrementMax=18}
::ab[Plane of Shadow]{icon=power-boost next ability="The planar scout gains darkvision 60 feet and a +4 bonus on Stealth checks." increment="The bonus on Stealth checks~4~4~4~2"}
::ab[Plane of Water]{icon=power next ability="The planar scout gains the ‹umr/amphibious› subtype and a swim speed equal to base speed."}

::ab[Planar Adaptation (Su)]{l=9 icon=def-protect passive="A planar scout gains ‹spell/planar adaptation› when on his bonded plane." imp16="He confers this protection to all allies within a 30-foot ‹misc/spread›." replace="Evasion~improved evasion"}
::hl3[rule/Planar Terrains]

A staggering array of terrains await exploration in the Great Beyond. Some are akin to the forests, mountains, oceans, and plains of Material Plane worlds, but others are unlike anything found there, such as the ghostly reaches of the Ethereal Plane, the living and horrific reaches of the deep Abyss, the constantly burning inferno of the Plane of Fire, and the life-draining nothingness of the Negative Energy Plane.

Some classes, like the ranger, interact directly with terrain, and class abilities like *favored terrain* can become complicated in a plane-hopping campaign. In games that are largely limited to the Material Plane, the planes terrain category works fine, but if you intend to run a game set on another plane, you should consider breaking up that category into specific planes (thus, a ranger would choose Heaven or Ethereal Plane, or even a demiplane like Leng as a *favored terrain*), or simply doing away with the planes category entirely. With this latter solution, you should work with ranger players to help them anticipate the terrains featured in your upcoming campaign, with new categories like crystalline (for realms in the Plane of Earth or under Elysium, where immense crystal formations are common), living (for truly sentient realms like portions of the Abyss), or void (for empty reaches in the Astral Plane or the energy planes) potentially becoming new options for rangers to select from.

poison_darter.name: Poison Darter
description: ## Poison Darter
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/9›
In dense jungles, foliage and tight spaces hinder all but the most deceptively humble weapons: ‹eq-weapon/blowgun«s› and poison.

:::ab{title="Debilitating Venom (Ex)" icon=power order=unC action=Ability flavor="A poison darter can concoct potent toxins that he can apply to his weapons." usage="A number of times/day equal to ‹misc/half› his ranger level + his Wisdom modifier" note="If he has the ‹race/toxic skin alternate racial trait»<grippli›, he can expend a daily use of that poison to employ his *debilitating venom* ability an additional time." imp5="The frequency increases to 6 rounds, and the toxin ‹misc/sicken«s› a creature for 1 round every time it fails its saving throw to resist the poison." imp10="The the poison's Dexterity damage increases to 1d4, and two successful saving throws are needed to cure the poison." imp15="The poison dart can choose to deal Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution damage with the *debilitating venom,* selected when he creates the toxin." imp20="The poison's damage die increases to 1d6." replace="Favored enemy~master hunter"}

The *debilitating venom* functions only for the poison darter and becomes inert if not used within 1 hour. Creating a *debilitating venom* is a @HL[standard action], and it can be applied to a weapon as a @HL[move action].
::aff[Debilitating Venom]{iconP type=Injury dcF dcLev="the poison darter" dcIt=his dcMod=Wis freqR=4 effDex="1d3" cure1}
::ab[Poison Use (Ex)]{icon=def ability="A poison darter is trained in the use of poison and cannot accidentally poison himself when applying poison to a weapon." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Poison Style (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="In place of a combat style feat, a poison darter can select any ‹ability/rogue talent<s› that modifies sneak attacks or any alchemist ‹ability/discovery› that modifies poisons he creates and applies, using his Wisdom modifier in place of his Intelligence modifier and his ranger level in place of his alchemist or rogue level for the purpose of meeting prerequisites. He can use the ‹discovery/sticky poison› discovery to apply a single dose of poison to two blowgun darts without reducing its potency." alter="Combat style feat"}

::ab[Precise Dart (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A poison darter gains a ‹ability/sneak attack› identical to the rogue class feature, but only when attacking with a blowgun. This deals 1d6 extra points of damage." incrementPlain="The sneak attack damage~4~2" incrementEnd="d6 damage." replace="Hunter's bond"}

raven_master.name: Raven Master
description: ## Raven Master
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/175›

While the ‹rule/Silver Ravens› adopted their name as a way to honor the group of adventurers who defended Kintargo during the Chelish Civil War, some Silver Ravens have gone even farther, breeding and training actual ravens to serve both as living symbols of the organization and as fearsome weapons against House Thrune and its diabolic servants. These rangers, known as raven masters, eschew a ranger's usual connection to the natural world to form a mystic bond with their birds and to grant both animal and master a number of specialized abilities. Over time, the feathers of these raven companions take on a silvery sheen, causing them to resemble the birds produced by the figurines of wondrous power, which served as the original Silver Ravens' namesake.

Raven masters serve as messengers and spies for the Silver Ravens, using their ravens to scout the border between Ravounel and Cheliax and report any suspicious Thrune troop movements. Outside of Cheliax, raven companions alight on the rooftops and window ledges of palaces and keeps to eavesdrop on tyrants and dictators, feeding these precious secrets to the raven master so that she can more efficiently foment and organize rebellion.
::cskill{gain=bluff~dis~soh lose=climb~heal~intm~ride~swim}
::ab[Avian Empathy (Ex)]{icon=down note="A raven master's *wild empathy* affects only birds." alter="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Raven Companion (Ex)]{l=4 head icon=power ability="A raven master earns the trust and companionship of a particularly large, powerful, and intelligent raven. This ability functions like the druid ‹ability/animal companion› ability, except that the raven master's effective druid level is equal to her ranger level - 3. The raven master must select a ‹companion/bird› as her animal companion. The raven companion starts with an Intelligence score of 3 and can speak one language known by the raven master. In addition to the normal abilities an ‹sidekick/animal companion› gains as the raven master increases in level, the raven companion gains the following abilities." alter="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Silver Champion (Ex)]{next icon=boost l7="The raven companion takes on a silvery, metallic appearance. It treats all of its natural attacks as though they were magic and silver for the purpose of bypassing damage reduction." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Mimic Figurine (Su)]{next icon=magic l8="Once per day as a @HLstandard_action, the raven master can transform her raven companion into a token similar to a ‹magic-wondrous/figurine of wondrous power›. Any gear worn by the raven melds into its body as part of this transformation, but held items do not. The raven can remain in this form indefinitely, or until the raven master spends a standard action to activate the statuette as though it were a *figurine of wondrous power.*" replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Cheat Death (Sp)]{next icon=def l12="Once per week, when a raven companion would otherwise be slain by an attack, spell, or effect, it can turn into a *figurine of wondrous power* as per its *mimic figurine* ability as an @HLimmediate_action. Once transformed this way, the raven companion cannot transform back into a raven until the raven master next prepares spells." replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Argent Magic]{icon=learn ability="A raven master casts spells as do most rangers, but her spell list is somewhat expanded from the normal options most rangers can choose from, for her association with the bards and other talented performers among the Silver Ravens allows her to add a limited number of ‹main/bard spell«s› to her spell list. These spells function as divine spells, and are cast and prepared in the same manner as her other ranger spells." imp6="The raven master selects one 1st-level bard spell of her choice and adds it to her spell list as a 1st-level ranger spell." imp9="She does the same but for a 2nd-level bard spell." imp12="She adds a 3rd-level bard spell." imp15="She adds a 4th-level bard spell." alter=Spellcasting}

realm_wanderer.name: Realm Wanderer
description: ## Realm Wanderer
‹SOURCE Distant Realms/22›

Some of the most free-roaming rangers are those who tread between locales so varied that they may as well be separate worlds altogether - and quite often these disparate places are literally ‹rule/Hell› and the Material Plane. Like the Queen of Dis, whom many of them admire, these rangers take to heart the lessons they've learned from their vastly contrasting experiences and know that creatively embracing this wisdom is sometimes the difference between life and death.
::cskill{gain=diplo~bluff lose=intm~stl}

::ab[Deceptive Subtlety (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A realm wanderer adds half his level (minimum 1) to Diplomacy checks to influence a creature's attitude, to make requests of creatures, and for similar uses of the skill. He also adds half his level (minimum 1) to Bluff checks to deceive, lie, or convey secret messages." replace=Track}
:::ab{l=4 icon=down title="Queen's Bond (Ex)" action=Ability alter="Hunter's bond"}

A realm wanderer must choose an animal companion for his *hunter's bond.* This ‹ability/animal companion› gains the ‹template/fiendish› template, likely either due to time it spent near a gate to Hell or due to infernal influences in its place of birth. Due to this insidious influence, this ‹sidekick/animal companion› always takes a penalty on attack rolls equal to ‹misc/one-fifth› of the realm wanderer's level (minimum 1).

If the realm wanderer releases his animal companion from service or it perishes, he can gain a new fiendish animal companion by completing the required ceremony. Alternatively, when the realm wanderer releases his animal companion or it dies in his service, he can use his influence over its fiendish nature to cause the animal to lose the fiendish template. If the animal companion is then raised or otherwise brought back from the dead after the realm wanderer removes its fiendish template, the animal companion no longer takes the penalty on attacks described above. (He cannot, however, gain a new non-fiendish animal companion by completing the ceremony to replace an animal companion.)

::ab[Dual Master (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost passive="The realm wanderer's bonuses from *deceptive subtlety* are doubled when he is in either of his two *favored terrain* types. However, this benefit applies only to the first two *favored terrain* types he selected; as he gains additional *favored terrains* at higher levels, this benefit does not apply in those additional terrain types." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Shrewdest Monarch (Su)]{l=20 icon=power-boost flavor="The realm wanderer has become a true master of the territories with which he is familiar." standard="He can make a single attack at his full attack bonus against an enemy in one of the terrains chosen using the *dual master* ability. If the attack hits, the target takes damage normally and must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw or die. The DC of this saving throw is equal to 10 + half the realm wanderer's level + his Wisdom modifier. A realm wanderer can instead deal an amount of nonlethal damage equal to the creature's current hit points; the target can attempt a Fortitude save at the same DC to negates this damage." usage="Four times/day, but not against the same creature more than once in a 24-hour period" passive="The realm wanderer also gains a bonus on attack rolls equal to his Wisdom modifier against creatures that have successfully learned his alignment (using ‹spell/detect «chaos/»evil«/good/law›, as appropriate, or similar effects)." order=usp replace="Master hunter"}

sable_company_marine.name: Sable Company Marine
description: ## Sable Company Marine
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/43;Paizo Blog - Sable Company Marine›

Sable Company marines receive their training at the elite Endrin Military Academy in Korvosa. A large portion of their education is the handling and riding of hippogriffs, the iconic mounts of members of the company.

::ab[Hippogriff Companion]{l=2 icon=power replace="Hunter's bond (but see text)" ability="A Sable Company marine adds ‹feat/Monstrous Mount› to the list of bonus feats made available to him by his chosen ‹ability/combat style<s›, regardless of the style chosen. He can only use this feat to select a ‹companion/hippogriff› mount. If he does so, he treats his ranger level - 1 as his effective druid level. **A marine who takes this option does not gain the *hunter's bond* class feature at 4th level.**" imp6="A Sable Company marine adds ‹feat/Monstrous Mount Mastery› to the list of available bonus feats granted by his chosen combat style. If he takes this feat as his 6th-level combat style bonus feat, note that he must wait until 8th level before he meets the effective druid level prerequisite to utilize his mount's *mastery* abilities."}

sentinel.name: Sentinel
description: ## Sentinel
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/42›

A sentinel specializes in heightening all of her senses to expose spies and those who work for opposing factions, rooting out all who attempt to slip past her evervigilant watch. Her uncanny skills are in ready demand by city guards, business owners, and even criminal organizations that desire to keep the authorities out of their disreputable affairs.

::ab[Mugshot (Sp)]{icon=boost ability="A sentinel can spend 1 minute studying the appearance of a target or a likeness of the target in the form of a picture, carving, or visual illusion and memorize it with perfect recall. From that point on, the sentinel gains a +4 bonus on Perception checks to notice the target in disguise or pick him out of a crowd. The sentinel is automatically allowed a Perception check against the target's Disguise check whenever she sees the target, even if the individual isn't doing anything to draw attention to himself." special="A sentinel can memorize the appearance of a number of targets equal to her Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) at any given time. Once a sentinel has reached her maximum number of memorized appearances, she must forget one of the memorized faces before gaining a new one." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Uncanny Alertness (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def-power flavor="A sentinel is always ready to defend against incoming attacks." passive="A sentinel is never ‹rule/flat-footed› before acting in combat, even if she is surprised (she can still become flat-footed by any other effect or situation that would render her flat-footed)." imp7="The sentinel also always acts on the surprise round. If she would have acted on the surprise round anyway, or if there is no surprise round, she gains a bonus on initiative checks equal to half her ranger level." replace="Hunter's bond~woodland stride"}

::ab[Sense Intruder (Su)]{l=6 icon=aura flavor="A sentinel gains the ability to establish an incredibly effective watch by standing still and focusing on all of her senses as once." ability="At any time, she can spend 1 minute setting up this ability. Once she has done so, she must stand still, taking no other actions except to keep track of her surroundings; she can't even take free actions like talking. Her heightened senses grant her a supernatural awareness of her surroundings within 20 feet. If any Tiny or larger creature enters the area over which her awareness extends, she automatically knows that it did so (this part of the ability is a divination effect and can't be deceived by spells that block sensory input, only by extreme effects like ‹spell/mind blank›). Furthermore, unless that creature is simultaneously negating its visual, auditory, and scent stimuli (such as by being under ‹spell/invisibility›, ‹spell/silence›, and ‹spell/negate aroma› all at once), the sentinel can attempt a Perception check as an @HLimmediate_action to pinpoint the intruder's location, ignoring any Stealth bonuses the intruder might gain from effects that obscure only one of those senses, such as invisibility, camouflage, or other spells or abilities." imp10="Starting at 10th level, whenever the sentinel gains a combat style feat, she can choose to trade it for a 10-foot increase in the radius of this ability." special="This sort of heightened analysis of sensory stimuli is taxing, as much so as ‹rule/hustling› overland for an equivalent period of time, despite not moving from a single spot. The sentinel can use this ability for 1 hour without penalty. Using *sense intruder* for a second hour in between sleep cycles deals 1 point of nonlethal damage to the sentinel, and each additional hour deals twice the damage taken during the previous hour. A sentinel who takes any nonlethal damage from using this ability becomes ‹misc/fatigued›. Each hour the sentinel uses *sense intruder* also counts as 1 hour of hustling overland for the purpose of determining how much nonlethal damage the sentinel takes in a single day." replace="Combat style feat gained at 6th level" alter="Combat style feats"}

::ab[Mugshot Quarry (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="The sentinel can activate her *quarry* ability (and her *improved quarry* ability at 19th level) against any of the targets she has memorized with the *mugshot* ability, even if the creature in question is not within her line of sight." alter="Quarry~improved quarry"}

shapeshifter.name: Shapeshifter
description: ## Shapeshifter
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/126›

Most rangers venture into the wilderness, but there are some who let the wilderness seep into them. Whether by curse, disease, ancient rite, a slight lycanthropic influence in the blood, or the corrupting influence of chaos, these rangers embrace the wild to transform themselves into something untamed and feral. Shapeshifters are often held in awe, but are even more often feared. A shapeshifter has the following class features.
::ab[Combat Style Feat (Ex)]{l=2 icon=down ability="A shapeshifter ranger must choose the natural weapon ‹ability/combat style<s›."}

::ab[Shifter's Blessing (Su)]{head l=3 icon=magic swift="The shapeshifter can take on the aspects of a wild creature once per day. He can remain in this form for a number of rounds equal to his ranger level + his Wisdom modifier. While in one of his shifter's blessing forms, the ranger gains the ‹subtype/shapechanger› subtype." order=wc choice="The shapeshifter must choose one of the following forms. Once this choice is made, it cannot be changed." incrementPlain="The ranger may select an additional form for his *shifter's blessing* and may use this~3~5" incrementDesc=ability incrementEnd=" times per day." special="This is not a polymorph ability; a ranger with *shifter's blessing* in one of his shifted forms can be affected by a polymorph ability and retain his bonus and traits gained by the class feature." replace="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Form of the Bear]{next icon=boost-down ability="The ranger's muscles enlarge and tighten, and his facial features become more ursine. While in this form, the ranger gains a +4 enhancement bonus to Strength, but his base speed becomes 20 feet."}

::ab[Form of the Cat]{next icon=boost ability="The ranger's muscles become lean and defined, and his gait more deliberate and graceful. While in this form, the ranger increases his base speed by 10 feet, and he gains a +4 bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks."}
::ab[Form of the Dragon]{next icon=def ability="The ranger's skin becomes rough and scaly. While in this form, the ranger gains a +2 natural armor bonus to AC."}
::ab[Form of the Eagle]{next icon=boost ability="The ranger's skin stretches, his nose becomes hooked, and his eyes enlarge. While in this form, the ranger gains a +10 bonus on Perception checks."}

::ab[Form of the Jackal]{next icon=power-def ability="The ranger becomes thin and hyperactive, his movements taking on a nervous spryness. While in this form, the ranger can spend a @HLmove_action to move up to 1/2 his speed without provoking attacks of opportunity."}

::ab[Form of the Otter]{next icon=power-boost ability="The ranger's hands and feet grow webs, and his body grows an oily fur that is water resistant. While in this form, the ranger gains a swim speed of 30 feet and a +8 bonus on Swim checks."}

::ab[Dual Form Shifter (Ex)]{l=12 icon=boost ability="When the shapeshifter takes on a *shifter's blessing* form, he can take on a hybrid of two of his forms. He gains the bonuses for both forms." replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Master Shifter (Su)]{head l=20 icon=boost ability="The ranger's *shifter's blessing* forms improve, and he can take on true forms of beasts. The ranger can use *dual form shifter* with this ability, although he cannot use more than one polymorph effect at any one time. The ranger's forms from *shifter's blessing* improve to the following." replace="Master hunter "}

::ab[Form of the Bear]{next icon=boost-magic ability="The ranger's muscles enlarge and tighten, and his facial features become more ursine. While in this form, the ranger gains a +8 enhancement bonus to Strength and he does not suffer any reduction of speed. Alternatively, the ranger can polymorph into the form of a bear as if the caster of ‹spell/beast shape IV›."}

::ab[Form of the Cat]{next icon=boost-magic ability="The ranger's muscles become lean and defined, and his gait more deliberate and graceful. While in this form, the ranger increases his base speed by 20 feet and he gains a +10 bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks. Alternatively, the ranger can polymorph into the form of a cat of any size as if the caster of *beast shape IV.*"}

::ab[Form of the Dragon]{next icon=def-power-magic ability="The ranger's skin becomes rough and scaly. While in this form, the ranger gains a +4 natural armor bonus to AC and a fly speed of 30 feet with average maneuverability. Alternately, the ranger can polymorph into the form of a dragon as if the caster of ‹spell/form of the dragon I›."}

::ab[Form of the Eagle]{next icon=boost-power-magic ability="The ranger's skin stretches, his nose becomes hooked, and his eyes enlarge. While in this form, the ranger gains a +10 bonus on Perception checks and a fly speed of 40 feet with good maneuverability. Alternatively, the ranger can polymorph into the form of an eagle or similar raptor as if the caster of *beast shape IV.*"}

::ab[Form of the Jackal]{next icon=def-magic ability="The ranger becomes thin and hyperactive, his movements taking on a nervous spryness. While in this form, the ranger does not provoke attacks of opportunity due to movement. Alternatively, the ranger can polymorph into the form of a jackal or other canine creature as if the caster of *beast shape IV.*"}

::ab[Form of the Otter]{next icon=power-boost-magic ability="The ranger's hands and feet grow webs, and his body grows an oily fur that is water resistant. While in this form, the ranger gains a swim speed of 60 feet and a +5 bonus on Swim checks. Alternatively, the ranger can polymorph into the form of an otter or similar river mammal as if the caster of *beast shape IV.*"}

skirmisher.name: Skirmisher
description: ## Skirmisher
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/128›

Many rangers rely on spells, but there are some who eschew aid from divine powers for their own reasons. Skirmishers rely on their wits, their wisdom, and sometimes even instinct to aid in their quests. A skirmisher has the following class features.

::ab[Hunter's Tricks]{head icon=power flavor="A skirmisher ranger learns the use of *hunter's tricks,* which typically grant a boon or bonus to the ranger or a nearby ally." l5="The ranger learns one trick, selected from the list below." incrementOrd="He learns~7~2" incrementDesc=his incrementEnd=" trick." usage="A number of times per day equal to 1/2 his ranger level + his Wisdom modifier" special="Tricks are usually @HLswift_actions, but sometimes @HLmove or @HLfree actions that modify a @HLstandard_action, usually an attack action. Once a trick is chosen, it can't be retrained. A ranger cannot select an individual trick more than once." replace="Spellcasting ability"}

::ab[Aiding Attack (Ex)]{next icon=boost free="The ranger can use this trick when he hits a creature with an attack. The next ally who makes an attack against the target creature before the start of the ranger's next turn gains a +2 circumstance bonus on that attack roll."}

::ab[Bolster Companion (Ex)]{next icon=protect immediate="The ranger can use this trick when his animal companion is hit with an attack or a combat maneuver. The companion's AC and combat maneuver defense increase by +4 for the purposes of that attack. If the attack still hits, the animal companion only takes half damage (if any). The animal companion must be able to see and hear the ranger to benefit from this trick."}

::ab[Catfall (Ex)]{next icon=def immediate="The ranger can use this trick when he falls 20 or more feet, ignoring the first 20 feet of the fall when calculating the ‹rule/falling« damage›. If the ranger takes no damage from the fall, he does not fall prone."}

::ab[Chameleon Step (Ex)]{next icon=power-boost move="The ranger can move up to twice his speed. The ranger does not take any penalty on Stealth checks for movement during this move. This move provokes attacks of opportunity as normal."}

::ab[Cunning Pantomime (Ex)]{next icon=power standard="The ranger can communicate with a single creature as if using the ‹spell/tongues› spell for 10 minutes. Because the communication is slow and lacks subtlety, the ranger suffers a -4 penalty on all Bluff and Diplomacy checks relating to the creature he is communicating with when using this trick."}
::ab[Defensive Bow Stance (Ex)]{next icon=def swift="Until the start of his next turn, the ranger's ranged attacks do not provoke attacks of opportunity."}
::ab[Deft Stand (Ex)]{next icon=def move="The ranger can spend a move action to stand up without provoking attacks of opportunity."}
::ab[Distracting Attack (Ex)]{next icon=lower free="The ranger can use this trick before he makes an attack. If the attack hits, the target takes a -2 penalty on all attack rolls for 1 round."}

::ab[Hateful Attack (Ex)]{next icon=boost free="The ranger can use this trick when he makes an attack against one of his *favored enemies.* The ranger doubles the threat range of his weapon for this attack. This does not stack with other abilities that increase a weapon's threat range."}

::ab[Heel (Ex)]{next icon=protect immediate="The ranger can use this trick when his animal companion moves. When the companion moves, the square it starts out in is not considered threatened by any opponent it can see, and therefore visible enemies do not get attacks of opportunity against it when it moves from that square. The companion must end this movement in a square adjacent to the ranger. The animal companion must be able to see and hear the ranger to take this movement."}
::ab[Hobbling Attack (Ex)]{next icon=lower free="The ranger can use this trick when he hits with an attack. The target of the attack's land speed is reduced by 1/2 for 1d4 rounds."}
::ab[Quick Climb (Ex)]{next icon=boost move="The ranger can climb at his full speed without penalty."}

::ab[Quick Healing (Ex)]{next icon=aid swift="The ranger can make a Heal check to administer first aid on an adjacent dying character." move="Alternatively, the ranger can administer a potion to an unconscious character." order=wm}
::ab[Quick Swim (Ex)]{next icon=boost move="The ranger can swim at his full speed without penalty."}

::ab[Ranger's Counsel (Ex)]{next icon=boost swift="The ranger can grant all allies within 30 feet that are within line of sight and can hear him a +2 bonus on skill checks with a single skill of his choice. The ranger must have at least one rank in the chosen skill. This bonus lasts for 1 round."}
::ab[Rattling Strike (Ex)]{next icon=lower free="The ranger can use this trick before he makes a melee attack. If the attack hits, the target is shaken for 1d4 rounds."}
::ab[Second Chance Strike (Ex)]{next icon=roll immediate="When he misses with a melee attack, the ranger may reroll his attack at a -5 penalty."}

::ab[Sic-Em (Ex)]{next icon=power swift="The ranger's animal companion makes one melee attack against an adjacent creature. The animal companion must be able to see and hear the ranger to make this attack."}
::ab[Skill Sage (Ex)]{next icon=roll free="The ranger can roll twice on any one skill check and take the better result. He must have at least 1 rank in that skill to use this ability."}
::ab[Stag's Leap (Ex)]{next icon=power free="The ranger can attempt a running jump without moving 10 feet before the jump."}
::ab[Surprise Shift (Ex)]{next icon=power swift="The ranger can move 5 feet. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity and does not count as a 5-foot step."}
::ab[Tangling Attack (Ex)]{next icon=lower free="The ranger can use this attack when he makes an attack. If the attack hits, the target is entangled for 1 round."}
::ab[Trick Shot (Ex)]{next icon=ranged standard="The ranger can make a ranged attack that ignores ‹rule/concealment› (but not total concealment), soft ‹rule/cover›, and partial cover."}
::ab[Uncanny Senses (Ex)]{next icon=boost immediate="The ranger gains a +10 insight bonus on Perception checks for 1 round."}

::ab[Upending Strike (Ex)]{next icon=power free="The ranger can use this trick just before he makes a melee attack. If the attack hits, he may make a free ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver against the target."}

::ab[Vengeance Strike (Ex)]{next icon=power immediate="The ranger can use this trick when an enemy adjacent to him hits an ally with a melee or ranged attack. The ranger can make a single melee attack at his highest base attack bonus against the creature who attacked his ally."}

spirit_ranger.name: Spirit Ranger
description: ## Spirit Ranger
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/129›

Some rangers nurture a connection with the spirits that reside in all things. By communing with these spirits, the spirit ranger can gain glimpses of things to come. A spirit ranger has the following class features.

::ab[Spirit Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="Instead of forming a bond with his hunting companions or an animal companion, the spirit ranger forms a bond with the spirits of nature themselves. Each day, as long as he is within one of his *favored terrains,* the ranger can cast ‹spell/augury› as a spell-like ability with a caster level equal to his ranger level." order=a2 ability2="In addition, he can call upon these spirits to cast any one ranger spell that he is capable of casting, without having to prepare the spell." incrementOrd="He can cast~8~4" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd=" additional spell in this way." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Wisdom of the Spirits (Sp)]{l=12 icon=boost ability="The spirit ranger can use his *augury* spell-like ability even when he is not in one of his *favored terrains.* If he is within one of his *favored terrains,* the ranger can cast ‹spell/divination› instead. Like *augury,* the caster level of the divination is equal to the ranger's level." replace=Camouflage}

stormwalker.name: Stormwalker
description: ## Stormwalker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/72›
Rangers who walk in the tempest unafraid draw the power of the storm into themselves and become stormwalkers.
::ab[Combat Style Feat (Ex)]{l=2 icon=down ability="A stormwalker must choose the archery ‹ability/combat style<s›." alter="Combat style"}

::ab[Thundershot (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="A stormwalker can wreathe his weapon in lightning." standard="He can grant a single weapon he holds the ‹magic-enh/shock› special ability for 1 minute; while under this effect, the weapon counts as magic for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." useMod=Wisdom3 imp12="Weapons affected by this ability gain the ‹magic-enh/shocking burst› special ability, instead." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Wind Treader (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def-boost passive="A stormwalker becomes immune to the effects of strong ‹rule/wind<s›. He can move without hindrance in windy conditions, and his ranged attacks take no penalties from nonmagical wind." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Flash Step (Su)]{l=11 icon=power-ranged flavor="A stormwalker can move across the battlefield like a bolt of lightning." fullround="He can transform into lightning and move to any unoccupied square within 60 feet without provoking attacks of opportunity. He can make a single ranged attack before or after this movement." replace=Quarry}
::ab[Control Weather (Sp)]{l=16 icon=magic ability="A stormwalker can cast ‹spell/control weather› as a spell-like ability once per day." replace="Improved evasion"}

::ab[Flash Shot (Su)]{l=19 icon=ranged ability="When a stormwalker uses *flash step,* he can make a ranged attack both before and after his movement. Both of these attacks are made at his highest base attack bonus." replace="Improved quarry"}

summit_sentinel.name: Summit Sentinel
description: ## Summit Sentinel
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/72›
Some rangers eschew the path of the swift skirmisher and embrace the implacable strength of the mountain.

::ab[Roots of the Mountain (Su)]{l=2 icon=power-def ability="A summit sentinel gains ‹feat/Toughness› as a bonus feat." move="She can enter a defensive stance for 1 minute. While in this stance, the summit sentinel's speed is reduced to 5 feet, she gains a +2 enhancement bonus to her natural armor, and she gains a +2 bonus to her CMD against maneuvers that would move her or knock her prone. If the ranger loses contact with the ground or other solid surface, the stance ends immediately." useMod=Constitution3 order=amu incrementMulti="The enhancement bonus to natural armor increases to +~ and the bonus to CMD becomes +~.~2/4;3/1;4/2" replace="Combat style feats"}

::ab[Mountain Mastery (Ex)]{l=3 icon=down note="A summit sentinel must select mountains as her *favored terrain,* and she does not gain additional *favored terrains* at higher levels." increment="The bonus from this *favored terrain*~5~5~4~2" imp8="A summit sentinel draws strength from the earth. When she and her target are both in contact with the ground or an earthen floor, the summit sentinel gains a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls against the target." imp18="The bonus on attack and damage rolls increases to +2." alter="Favored terrain"}
::ab[Spells]{icon=learn ability="A summit sentinel adds ‹spell/stoneskin› to the ranger spell list as a 4th-level spell." alter=Spells}

::ab[Rockslide (Su)]{l=8 icon=cone standard="Once per day, a summit sentinel can create a rushing wave of earth and stone. The summit sentinel chooses a point on the ground or floor within 30 feet. Rippling waves of earth and stone emanate from the chosen point in a 25-foot cone in a direction of the summit sentinel's choice. Each creature in the area takes 4d6 points of bludgeoning damage and is pushed to the end of the cone. A target that succeeds at a Reflex save (DC = 10 + half the ranger's level + the ranger's Wisdom modifier) takes half damage and is not pushed." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Invincible (Su)]{l=17 icon=def flavor="A summit sentinel takes on the unyielding hardness of the mountains." passive="While in her *roots of the mountain* defensive stance, she is immune to critical hits and ‹misc/precision damage›." replace="Hide in plain sight"}

sword_devil.name: Sword-Devil
description: ## Sword-Devil
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Worldscape #1/27›

Agile, vengeful, and deadly, a sword-devil fights with precision and grace, channeling the difficult lessons of a star-crossed life into unparalleled battle prowess. Favoring speed and evasive techniques over brute force, sword-devils choose precision over power and acrobatics over armor. Charismatic and carefree, a sword-devil makes fast friends and inspires her companions to greatness, even while vowing a swift death upon her enemies.
::cskill{gain=acro lose=spc}

::ab[Death Vow (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost useInc=ranger~3~1 useF="1~1~3" swift="A sword-devil may focus all her fury and determination on a single foe she swears to slay. The sword-devil gains a bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against her sworn target equal to half her sword-devil level (minimum +1). The *death vow* effect remains until the target of the *death vow* is dead, or the next time the sword-devil rests and regains use of this ability." replace="Favored enemy"}

::ab[Slashing Fury (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="The sword-devil can use her Charisma score in place of her Intelligence score as a prerequisite for combat feats." order=pa ability="She selects one type of light or one-handed slashing melee weapon. When using this weapon, she may use her Dexterity modifier instead of her Strength modifier on melee attack rolls." repeat="The sword-devil may select an additional type of light or one-handed slashing weapon she may use with this ability.~8~5" special="This ability counts as having the ‹feat/Weapon Finesse› feat for the purpose of meeting feat prerequisites." replace="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Inspiring Example (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="The sword-devil forms a bond with her companions, inspiring them to greater zeal in combat by the example of her weaponcraft." standard="The sword-devil may grant half her *death vow* bonus against a single victim of her *death vow* to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear her. This bonus lasts for a number of rounds equal to the sword-devil's Charisma modifier (minimum 1)." special="This bonus does not stack with any *death vow* bonuses possessed by the sword-devil's allies; they use whichever bonus is higher." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Untouchable (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def-warning flavor="The sword-devil's confidence and personality distract her foes in combat, making her more difficult to hit." ability="The sword-devil may add her Charisma bonus (if any) to her AC and her CMD." imp6="The sword-devil gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC." increment="The dodge bonus~6~3" special="She loses these bonuses when she wears any armor, when she carries a shield, or when she carries a medium or heavy load." replace="Spellcasting ability"}

::ab[Second Combat Style (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="The sword-devil chooses a second ranger ‹ability/combat style<s›. When she recieves a bonus combat feat, she may select any feat from either combat style's list." replace=Quarry}

::ab[Seething Fury (Ex)]{l=19 icon=boost flavor="The sword-devil's countless battles harden her soul and make her lose touch with her humanity. Her growing lack of control over her wrath fuels the potency of her *death vow.*" ability="She adds her Charisma bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against targets of her *death vow,* and automatically confirms threatened critical hits against them." replace="Improved quarry"}

::ab[Avatar of Vengeance (Su)]{l=20 icon=power-def flavor="A sword-devil becomes a living avatar of vengeance, infused with supernatural powers that push her beyond the mortal, and recognized for her battle prowess and unparalleled skill at killing by a potent otherworldly entity." note="The sword-devil becomes the living vessel of an immortal, intangible, and inscrutable being obsessed only with slaying her foes, meting out violent retribution to those who have wronged her, or simply dispatching her enemies in dazzling display of wanton destruction." standard="The sword-devil allows herself to be infused with this killing spirit. She gains a fly speed of 60-feet (perfect), a +6 morale bonus to AC, and fire resistance 30." order=nus usage="10 minutes per day, but it must be used in 1-minute increments." replace="Master hunter"}

tanglebriar_demonslayer.name: Tanglebriar Demonslayer
description: ## Tanglebriar Demonslayer
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/46›

During the elves' long absence from Golarion, the nascent demon lord ‹monster/Treerazer› took up residence in the Fierani Forest in what is now the Elven kingdom of Kyonin. Eventually, the demon's presence in the forest drew the elves back to Golarion, who sought to purge the fiendish blight from this once-sacred land. They defeated Treerazer but could not destroy him, forcing him instead into the southern reaches of the Fierani, a corrupted region of twisted plants and fiendish denizens called Tanglebriar. Even today, the ‹race/elves› constantly battle to keep the corruption in the Tanglebriar from spreading to the rest of the Fierani Forest and beyond. Though they are sworn foes of Treerazer and his kin, Tanglebriar demonslayers are known to venture forth from Kyonin to broaden their expertise in the destruction of demons.
::cskill{gain=kp}

::ab[Favored Enemy (Ex)]{icon=down-def l=1 replace="Favored enemy" choice="A Tanglebriar demonslayer must choose *favored enemy* (evil outsider)." note="Every time he advances his *favored enemy* bonus, the demonslayer must advance his bonus against evil outsiders." passive="In addition to the normal benefits of *favored enemy,* a Tanglebriar demonslayer gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his *favored enemy* bonus on saving throws against spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of evil outsiders." order=cnp special="This ability is otherwise identical to and replaces the *favored enemy* ranger class ability."}

::ab[Demonologist (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost flavor="A Tanglebriar demonslayer gains insight into his Abyssal enemies." passive="The demonslayer gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his class level on Knowledge (planes) checks that relate to demons, Perception checks to pierce a demon's disguise, and Survival checks while tracking demons. This bonus stacks with any bonuses from *favored enemy.*" replace=Endurance}

::ab[Expanded Spell List]{icon=learn ability="A Tanglebriar demonslayer adds the following spells to his spell list at the listed spell level." s1="Protection from evil" s2="Align weapon~~magic circle against evil" s4="Dimensional anchor~~dismissal"}

::ab[Fiendish Quarry (Su)]{l=11 icon=boost ability="When a Tanglebriar demonslayer chooses an evil outsider as his *quarry,* he can the forgo the normal +2 attack bonus and instead treat his weapon as if it were good-aligned versus his *quarry.*" replace=Quarry special="This ability is otherwise identical to *quarry* and replaces that ability."}

::ab[Improved Fiendish Quarry (Su)]{l=19 icon=boost replace="Improved quarry" ability="When a Tanglebriar demonslayer chooses an evil outsider as his *quarry,* he can forgo the normal +4 attack bonus and instead treat his weapon as if it had the ‹magic-enh/holy› weapon special ability while attacking his quarry." special="This ability is otherwise identical to *improved quarry* and replaces that ability."}

tidal_hunter.name: Tidal Hunter
description: ## Tidal Hunter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/73›
The tidal hunter draws strength from the fish and other wildlife of the deep.

::ab[Waterborn (Su)]{icon=power ability="A tidal hunter can breathe water for up to 10 minutes per ranger level each day." imp6="He can breathe underwater indefinitely." replace="Wild empathy~6th-level combat style feat"}

::ab[Keen Scent (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A tidal hunter gains ‹umr/scent› as per the universal monster rule." imp12="While in water, the tidal hunter can detect particularly strong or distinct odors (such as blood) up to 1 mile away." replace=Track~camouflage}

::ab[Aquatic Mastery (Ex)]{icon=down l3="A tidal hunter must select water as his *favored terrain,* and he doesn't gain additional *favored terrains.*" repeat="The bonus from this *favored terrain* increases by 2.~8~5" alter="Favored terrain"}
::ab[Wave Rush (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A tidal hunter gains a swim speed equal to his base speed. If he already has a swim speed, it increases by 10 feet." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Tidal Surge (Sp)]{l=16 icon=magic flavor="A tidal hunter gains control over water, and he can use the tides to attack his foes." ability="Once per day, he can cast ‹spell/tidal surge› as a spell-like ability." replace="Improved evasion"}

toxic_herbalist.name: Toxic Herbalist
description: ## Toxic Herbalist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/74›
Saviors and destroyers in equal measure, toxic herbalists use nature's bounty to craft remedies and poisons.

::ab[Toxic Touch (Ex)]{icon=def passive="A toxic herbalist studies the safe handling of deadly substances. He cannot accidentally poison himself when he applies poison to a weapon." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Nature's Mercy (Ex and Su)]{l=3 icon=power-aid order=a2 flavor="A toxic herbalist's knowledge of the natural world offers respite from wounds and illness." ability="Once per day, he can spend 1 hour in a wilderness area gathering herbs and other natural materials and combine them into a medicinal poultice - this hour can be integrated with the hour required for the ranger to prepare spells." ability2="Placing the poultice on a wound or ingesting it is a @HLstandard_action; the creature receiving the poultice heals a number of hit points equal to 1d6 + the ranger's Wisdom modifier." incrementPlain="The ranger can now~3~8" incrementDesc=create incrementEnd=" poultices per day." imp8="A poultice neutralizes one poison affecting the recipient, as per ‹spell/neutralize poison›." imp13="A poultice also attempts to cure one disease affecting the recipient, as per ‹spell/remove disease›." special="The caster level of the neutralizing/curing effects is equal to the toxic herbalist's level. These additional effects are supernatural effects." replace=Endurance}

::ab[Sudden Onset (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-learn swift="A toxic herbalist can administer a poultice he created via *nature's mercy* or poison a weapon as a swift action." order=wn note="Additionally, the toxic herbalist adds the following spells to the ranger spell list at the indicated spell levels." s1="Nauseating dart~~ray of sickening" s2="Pernicious poison~~pox pustules" s3="Poison~~toxic gift" s4="Absorb toxicity~~cloudkill" replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Grim Harvest (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A toxic herbalist can spend 1 hour foraging for poisonous substances to create a dose of poison (this hour can be integrated with the hour required to prepare spells or poultices). The toxic herbalist rolls 1d20 and adds his ranger level and his Intelligence modifier. The DC of this check is equal to the Craft DC of the poison he wishes to create. If he succeeds at this check, the ranger creates 1 dose of the chosen poison. The poison remains potent for a number of hours equal to the toxic herbalist's level, regardless of the means used in attempts to preserve it, and it can't be sold." special="This ability functions only while the toxic herbalist is within one of his *favored terrains.*" replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Potent Venom (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost flavor="A toxic herbalist's poisons become even deadlier." ability="When he poisons a *favored enemy,* the DC of the poison's saving throw increases by half the toxic herbalist's *favored enemy* bonus against that target." replace=Quarry}

::ab[Poisonous Reaper (Ex)]{l=19 icon=boost passive="A single dose of poison on a toxic herbalist's weapon remains potent for the entirety of a full attack, instead of for a single strike." replace="Improved quarry"}

toxophilite.name: Toxophilite
description: ## Toxophilite
‹SOURCE Ranged Tactics Toolbox/11›

While many rangers use bows and arrows, the toxophilite takes special pride in her skill at archery. The toxophilite emulates the sharpshooting rangers of legend, who could pin a fly to the wall with a single shot or split one arrow with another. A truly talented toxophilite can even shoot down an enemy's arrow while it's still in flight.

::ab[Arrow-Splitter (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A toxophilite gains a +1 bonus on ranged attack rolls against creatures or objects two or more size categories smaller than her. The toxophilite's ranged attacks also deal full damage to objects instead of half damage." replace="Wild empathy"}
::ab[Combat Style Feat (Ex)]{l=2 icon=down choice="A toxophilite must select one of two ranged ‹ability/combat styles› to pursue: archery or crossbow."}

::ab[Intercept Arrow (Ex)]{l=3 icon=protect ability="A toxophilite can ‹rule/ready› a ranged attack to deflect a ranged weapon attack against her or an ally within 30 feet. To deflect the attack, the toxophilite makes an attack with a ranged weapon. If her attack roll is greater than the attacking opponent's roll, the opponent's initial attack automatically misses. For each size category the attacking creature is larger than the toxophilite, the toxophilite takes a -4 penalty on her attack roll." special="The toxophilite must declare the use of this ability after the attack is announced, but before the roll is made. Unusually massive ranged weapons, such as boulders or ballista bolts, and ranged attacks by firearms or generated by natural attacks or spell effects can't be deflected." replace=Endurance}

::ab[Improved Intercept (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost ability="When the toxophilite takes a full-attack action with a ranged weapon, she can choose to forgo one of her attacks. If she does so, she can use her *intercept arrow* ability as an @HLimmediate_action instead of a readied action at any time before her next turn. Her attack roll to intercept uses the same bonuses as the attack she chose to forgo during her previous action." replace=Quarry}

::ab[Greater Intercept (Ex)]{l=19 icon=ranged ability="Whenever a toxophilite successfully deflects an attack with her *intercept arrow* ability, she can redirect the attack against a target within 30 feet, using the intercepted attack's attack roll against the new target's AC." replace="Improved quarry"}

transporter.name: Transporter
description: ## Transporter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/43›

Transporters specialize in smuggling people as efficiently as possible through harsh and difficult environments. Individuals such as escaped political exiles, refugees fleeing danger, and various criminals all seek the transporter's services. While some transporters actively work to serve justice or a political cause, others are wily opportunists filling a need and profiting from the high price that desperate people are willing to pay for safe, discrete passage.

::ab[Trailbreaker (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="A transporter constantly works to obscure his trail." passive="He increases the Survival DC to track him, or a group of which he is a part, by an amount equal to half his ranger level (minimum 1). This does not stack with similar abilities, such as a ‹arc-inquisitor/traceless operative«'s› *trackless* ability." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Plot Course (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost ability="A transporter can spend 1d4 hours and attempt a DC 15 Knowledge (geography) check to plot a subtle course of travel between his current location and another destination. The transporter's course of travel is almost never direct, as it is optimized for smuggling. Thereafter, the transporter gains a +2 bonus on initiative, Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks involving traveling along his plotted course." incrementMulti="If the transporter's Knowledge (geography) check exceeds the DC by ~, the bonuses increase to +~.~3/5;10/10;4/2" replace="Favored terrain" special="A transporter can gain the benefits of only a single plotted course at any given time. If the transporter ever strays from the course or attempts to plot a new course for any reason, he loses the benefits of his current course immediately. A transporter can retry his Knowledge (geography) check to plot a course as long as he spends 1d4 hours each time."}

::ab[Smuggler's Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=down-boost order=np note="A transporter must choose his allies as his *hunter's bond,* not an animal companion." passive="Instead of granting the transporter's *favored enemy* bonus to his allies as a move action, he instead grants his *plot course* bonus to his allies constantly." alter="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Hideaway (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A transporter@FN1 can spend 1 hour and attempt a DC 20 Survival check to create a hidden shelter, useful for staying unseen by patrols of enemy creatures. The transporter's shelter can hold the transporter and one additional creature for every 2 by which the transporter exceeds the DC. The created shelter incorporates scents from the local area, preventing creatures on the inside from being detected by the ‹umr/scent› ability of creatures outside of the shelter, and vice versa. Additionally, its floor is either elevated or constructed of a different substance than the nearby ground, preventing creatures outside of the shelter from using ‹umr/tremorsense› to detect creatures inside, and vice versa. Finally, the clever structure of the hideaway causes creatures outside of the shelter to take a -10 penalty on Perception checks to notice creatures inside, as if through a 1-foot-thick wall, while only causing creatures inside to take a -2 penalty on Perception checks to notice creatures outside." special="These bonuses do not apply if the enemy creatures are intimately familiar with the area in which the hideaway was built (such as an area right near their home)." replace="Woodland stride"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[There is no listed level at which this ability is granted. Given that it replaces a 7th-level ability, the intention was probably that this ability would also be gained at 7th level. Consult your GM.]{from=1}

trapper.name: Trapper
description: ## Trapper
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/65›
A trapper is a ranger who focuses exclusively on traps, rather than learning conventional magic. A trapper has the following class features.
::cskill{gain=ddev}

::ab[Trapfinding]{icon=boost-power passive="A trapper adds 1/2 her ranger level on Perception skill checks made to locate traps and on Disable Device skill checks (minimum +1)." order=pa ability="A trapper can use Disable Device to disarm magic traps."}

::ab[Trap]{icon=power l5="A trapper learns how to create a ‹rangertrap/snare trap› and one other ‹ability/ranger trap<s› of her choice." repeat="She learns another trap.~7~2" special="The trapper can use these traps a total number of times per day equal to 1/2 her ranger level + her Wisdom modifier. Once a trap is learned, it can't be unlearned and replaced with a different type of trap. The trapper cannot select an individual trap more than once." replace="Spellcasting ability"}

::ab[Launch Trap]{l=10 icon=ranged ability="A trapper can affix a magical ranger trap to an arrow, crossbow bolt, or thrown weapon, allowing her to set the trap remotely or use it as a direct attack. Attaching the trap to the projectile is part of the @HLfull-round_action of creating a new trap. The trapped projectile is fired or thrown in the normal manner. If fired at a square, the trap is treated as if the ranger had set the trap in that square, except the DC is 5 lower than normal. If fired at a creature, the target takes damage from the ranged weapon and is treated as if it had triggered the trap (saving throw applies, if any). The attack has a maximum range of 60 feet, and range increments apply to the attack roll." special="The duration of the trapped projectile starts from when it is created, not from when it is used."}

trophy_hunter.name: Trophy Hunter
description: ## Trophy Hunter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/67›

Some rangers have taken up the mysteries of black powder in order to become big game hunters. Finding firearms useful for taking down large and dangerous prey, they enter the fight with the crack of black powder fire, often ranging far and wide in their safaris and searches for ever-more-dangerous denizens of the wild to track, study, appreciate - and kill. While this might strike some as going against the ranger's usual respect for nature and the creatures of the wild, trophy hunters understand that they are just more pieces in nature's grand puzzle, and that the laws of the jungle - the right to kill and feed on those weaker than you, and the need to establish dominance through cunning and bloodshed - are both their rights and their duty as the current top of the food chain.

::ab[Improved Tracking (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost-power ability="A trophy hunter gains a +2 bonus on Survival skill checks when following or identifying tracks. When he tracks, he can also attempt a Knowledge (nature) check at DC 15. On a success, the trophy hunter can discern the type and condition of any animals or magical beasts he tracks. By studying their tracks, the trophy hunter is able to identify a rough approximation of their health, maneuverability, and their general behavior as compared to the norm." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Firearm Style]{l=2 icon=power ability="A trophy hunter gains the ‹feat/Amateur Gunslinger› feat and ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency› (firearms), and can use any 1st-level ‹ability/gunslinger deed<s›." repeat="The trophy hunter can take a ‹main/grit feat› or select a gunslinger deed of his level or lower, ranger's choice.~6~4" replace="All combat style feats"}

::ab[Hunter's Aim (Ex)]{l=4 icon=zap flavor="A trophy hunter gains a specific understanding of the weaknesses and vulnerabilities of his *favored enemies,* and his careful study of these enemies reveals the best way to hurt them." ability="When the trophy hunter makes a firearm attack against a *favored enemy,* he can target touch AC in the first two range increments of his firearm." special="This ability stacks with other effects that increase the range increments to target touch AC, adding one range increment to the effect." replace="Hunter's bond"}

urban_ranger.name: Urban Ranger
description: ## Urban Ranger
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/130›
For the urban ranger, the streets and sewers of the city are just as dangerous as the barren wastelands or the deep forests. An urban ranger has the following class features.
::cskill{gain=ddev~kl lose=ha~kna}

::ab[Favored Community (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="The urban ranger forms a bond with a community. While inside the limits of this community, he gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (local), Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks. An urban ranger traveling through his favored community leaves no trail and cannot be tracked (although he may leave a trail if he so desires)." repeat="An urban ranger may select an additional favored community. In addition, the skill bonus and initiative bonus in any one favored community (including the one just selected, if so desired) increases by +2.~8~5" special="For the purposes of this ability, a community is any settlement consisting of 100 or more individuals. The community may be larger than this minimum. Outlying farms, fields, and houses are not considered part of a community." replace="Favored terrain"}
::ab[Trapfinding (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="An urban ranger can find and disable traps, as the rogue class feature of the ‹ability/same name»<trapfinding›." replace=Endurance}

::ab[Push Through (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost ability="An urban ranger is never slowed by ‹rule/difficult terrain› in his favored communities. In addition, he can move through the space occupied by local citizens as if they were allies. This does not apply to creatures intent on harming the ranger. Areas that are enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion, however, still affect him." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Blend In (Ex)]{icon=boost-power l12="An urban ranger can use his Stealth bonus in place of a Disguise skill check in any of his favored communities. This disguise does not take an action to don. He must make a check whenever someone attempts to pick him out from the local citizens. If his check is successful, he blends into the crowd. While not invisible, enemies do not notice his presence and take no actions against him unless they are taking actions against the local citizens in general." replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Invisibility Trick (Sp)]{l=17 icon=magic swift="The urban ranger can cast ‹spell/greater invisibility› on himself as a wizard of his ranger level." useMod=Wisdom useM=1 replace="Hide in plain sight"}

warden.name: Warden
description: ## Warden
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/68›

All rangers have a bond with the wilderness, but the warden may have the strongest. This protector sits guard in the middle of the wilderness, keeping a lookout for any dangers that might spill from the deeper wilderness beyond, and protecting civilization from the savagery of nature - and vice versa. His long weeks, months, and sometimes years of isolation cause him to "hear" what the land is saying. Not all are crazy.
::ab[Master of Terrain (Ex)]{icon=power l1="A warden selects his first *favored terrain.*" repeat="He gains another favored terrain.~3~5" replace="1st favored enemy"}

::ab[Live in Comfort (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power note="A warden is truly at home within his *favored terrains.* He knows all the signs of the wilderness. He rarely gets lost. He can easily live off the land in comfort, and can provide for companions." order=na ability="The warden can take 10 on any Survival check involving any of his *favored terrains,* even when in immediate danger and distracted. If the warden is not in immediate danger and distracted, he can take 20 even if the check has a penalty for failure." replace="All combat style feats"}

::ab[Terrain Bond (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-def flavor="A warden forms a bond with the land itself, enabling him to direct others in such terrain." passive="When in his *favored terrain,* the warden grants all allies within line of sight and that can hear him a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Perception, Stealth, and Survival skill checks. Also, as long as they travel with him, the warden's allies leave no trail and can't be tracked. The warden can choose for the group to leave a trail, or even for specific members of the group to leave a trail if he so desires." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Able Explorer (Ex)]{l=5 icon=roll passive="When making an Acrobatics, Climb, Fly, Ride, or Swim check in any of his *favored terrains,* a warden can make two skill checks and take the higher." replace="2nd, 3rd, and 4th favored enemies"}

::ab[Wilderness Whispers (Su)]{l=20 icon=power-def ability="A warden cannot be surprised and always acts as if he had rolled a natural 20 on any initiative check while within any of his *favored terrains.* A warden can always move at full speed while using Survival to follow tracks without penalty." replace="5th favored enemy~master hunter"}

wave_warden.name: Wave Warden
description: ## Wave Warden
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/194›
The wave warden patrols beneath the sea, preserving the safety and secrets of merfolk communities. Though he fares best beneath the water, dry land is no haven to his quarry.
::prereq{r=Merfolk}
::ab[Deep Sentinel (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A wave warden adds half his level (minimum +1) on Perception checks made to notice creatures underwater." replace=Track}

::ab[Aquatic Prowess Feat (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="And every four levels thereafter, a wave warden selects a bonus feat that improves his prowess in aquatic environments. He can choose these feats even if he does not meet the prerequisites. Initially, he may choose from the following feats: ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Mobility›, ‹feat/Net Adept›, ‹feat/Net and Trident›, ‹feat/Net Maneuvering›, ‹feat/Precise Shot›, ‹feat/Rapid Reload›, ‹feat/Sea Hunter›, and ‹feat/Two-Weapon Fighting›." repeat="He can select another bonus feat, following the rules above.~6~4" imp6="He adds ‹feat/Improved Two-Weapon Fighting›, ‹feat/Net Trickery›, and ‹feat/Spring Attack› to the list of feats." imp10="He adds ‹feat/Greater Two-Weapon Fighting› and ‹feat/Improved Precise Shot› to the list." special="This ability otherwise functions like and replaces the standard ranger's combat style bonus feats, including the limitations on armor worn." replace="All combat style bonus feats"}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=down replace="Favored terrain" ability="A wave warden gains water as a *favored terrain.* He does not gain additional *favored terrains.* This otherwise functions like the standard ranger's *favored terrain* ability and replaces that ability." repeat="His bonus in aquatic terrain increases by +2.~8~5"}

::ab[Seaborn (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A wave warden may move through any sort of aquatic growth (such as coral or seaweed) or across a wet surface at his normal speed and without taking damage or suffering any other impairment. Obstacles that are enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion still affect him." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Watery Summons (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic fullround="A wave warden can summon allies once per day. This functions as ‹spell/summon nature's ally III›, except it can only be used to summon creatures with the aquatic or water subtypes. The warden's caster level is equal to his ranger level." incrementRoman="This ability improves to ‹spell/summon nature's~11~3~4" incrementDesc=ally incrementEnd="›." replace="Swift tracker"}

wild_hunter.name: Wild Hunter
description: ## Wild Hunter
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/108›

A wild hunter seeks to emulate the animals around him to keep him safe while he tracks his prey. Instead of studying the traits and behaviors of a *favored enemy,* a wild hunter studies those of various animals, incorporating those attributes into his hunting strategy.

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{l=1 icon=power swift="A wild hunter can take on the ‹ability/aspect of an »animal<_foci›, gaining a bonus or special ability based on the type of animal emulated. This functions as the hunter's *animal focus* class feature, though this only applies to the wild hunter and not an animal companion (see *shared focus,* below)." useL=ranger useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="He can only emulate one animal at a time." replace="Favored enemy"}
