::ab[Shared Focus (Su)]{l=7 icon=boost ability="A wild hunter can share his current animal focus with one creature appropriate to his *hunter's bond.* If the wild *hunter's bond* is with an animal companion, the companion automatically gains the benefits of the wild hunter's current animal focus. If the wild *hunter's bond* is with his companions, as a @HLswift_action he can select one bonded ally to gain the benefits of the hunter's current animal focus; this lasts until the *hunter's bond* ends, the animal focus ends for the wild hunter, or the wild hunter selects a different companion." replace="Woodland stride~swift tracker"}

wild_shadow.name: Wild Shadow
description: ## Wild Shadow
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/45›

The isolation that some ‹race/half-elves› feel leads them to live a life of isolation amid the wild places of the world. Such rangers stalk the wild like shadows, creating close bonds with the wild itself instead of seeking the solace and aid of companions. While ill at ease within cities and other urban areas, they are adept at using the terrain to tactical advantage; they dart through brambles and rough terrain with uncommon grace and use the land itself to lock down enemies. A wild shadow has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Half-elf}

::ab[Wild at Heart (Ex)]{l=1 icon=down-boost passive="A wild shadow adds only 1/2 his class level when making *wild empathy* checks while in urban areas, and adds only 1/4 his class level to follow or identify tracks in such areas. In non-urban settings, he is considered two levels higher when determining the bonuses for such checks." alter="Track~wild empathy"}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=down note="When a wild shadow chooses a *favored terrain,* he cannot choose urban as the terrain type. Furthermore, when he chooses a new *favored terrain* type, he cannot choose the urban terrain type." alter="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Woodland Stride (Ex)]{icon=power l4="This ability functions as the 7th-level ranger class feature ‹ability/woodland_stride>«of the same name›, but the wild shadow gains it at 4th level instead." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Unfettered Step (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A wild shadow's *woodland stride* class feature functions in any ‹rule/difficult terrain› within any of his *favored terrains,* even in areas that are enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Harrying Attack (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power-lower flavor="A wild shadow can use his knowledge of terrain to make shrewd attacks in combat. Such attacks can make foes fumble or cause them to become entangled within areas of the wild shadow's *favored terrain.*" standard="The wild shadow denotes one target within line of sight and within one of his *favored terrains* as his *harried prey.* Once the foe is so designated, every time the wild shadow hits this *harried prey* with a melee or ranged weapon attack (either manufactured or natural), that creature is ‹rule/entangled› for 1 round. A wild shadow can have no more than one *harried prey* at a time and that creature must correspond to one of his *favored enemy* types." special="He can dismiss this effect at any time as a @HLfree_action, but he cannot select a new *harried prey* for 24 hours. If the wild shadow sees proof that his *harried prey* is dead, he can select a new *harried prey* after waiting 1 hour." replace=Quarry}

::ab[Wild Stalker (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def flavor="A wild shadow learns to better use natural surroundings to obscure his position in combat." passive="Whenever a wild shadow is within one of his *favored terrains* and a feature of that terrain grants him ‹rule/cover›, the bonuses to AC and Reflex saves for that cover improve by 1. Additionally, while he is within one of his *favored terrains,* if he gains ‹rule/concealment› or total concealment, the miss chance of either type of concealment improves by 10%." repeat="The bonuses granted by cover increase by 1 and the miss chance increases by another 10%.~16~3" replace=Camouflage}

::ab[Master of Terrain (Ex)]{l=19 icon=boost choice="A wild shadow can use his *harrying attack* against creatures other than his *favored enemy* or he can spend a @HLstandard_action to designate up to two of his *favored enemies* as his *harried prey* instead." replace="Improved quarry"}

wild_soul.name: Wild Soul
description: ## Wild Soul
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/75›

Hailing from primitive roots in the wild, some rangers venture to more civilized lands and learn their ways. Wild souls carry these more refined teachings back to the frontier lands of their birth, and they employ that knowledge in defense of their homes.

::ab[Unfettered Soul]{icon=warning note="A wild soul swears to himself never to make use of advanced technology, alchemy, arcane magic, or firearms. He also must oppose their use on his person, refusing to be a willing target and attempting a saving throw if possible (even if the effect is harmless or beneficial). Violating this oath causes a wild soul to lose the abilities granted by this archetype until he ‹spell/atone«s<ment›."}

::ab[Nemesis (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A wild soul gains a +2 bonus on Intimidate, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, Spellcraft, and Survival checks against creatures that wield advanced technology, alchemical weapons (including an alchemist's bombs), or firearms and against those that cast arcane spells (this does not apply to creatures that use spell-like abilities). Likewise, he gains a +2 bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against such opponents." order=pa ability="The wild soul can attempt Craft (alchemy), Knowledge (arcana), and Spellcraft checks untrained when attempting to identify enemy magic or advanced technology." increment="p!These bonuses~0~5~3" replace="Favored enemy"}

::ab[Nemesis Defense (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def passive="A wild soul adds his *nemesis* bonus on saving throws against advanced technology, alchemical items (including an alchemist's bombs and extracts), and arcane spells. He also adds this bonus to his AC against advanced technological items, alchemical items (including alchemist bombs), arcane spells that require attack rolls, and firearms." replace="Swift tracker"}

::ab[Break the Interloper (Su)]{l=11 icon=lower-aid ability="When a wild soul hits an arcane spellcaster, a creature with *extract* slots, or a creature with *grit* (but not grit-like pools such as *panache*) with a weapon attack, the target either loses its highest-level available spell or extract slot or loses 1 point of grit, and the wild soul gains 5 ‹rule/temporary hit points›." special="A target of this ability can negate this effect with a successful Fortitude save (DC = 10 + half the ranger's level + his Wisdom modifier). Regardless of the outcome of the saving throw, the target is immune to this ability for 24 hours." replace=Quarry}

::ab[Dizzying Onslaught (Ex)]{l=19 icon=lower flavor="A wild soul lands punishing blows that devastate his nemeses' ability to focus." ability="When he confirms a critical hit against a creature against whom his *nemesis* bonus applies, for 1 minute the target must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check (DC = 20 + the wild soul's Wisdom modifier + twice the spell's level) to cast an arcane spell, create an *extract,* or use an alchemist's bomb, and the target can't spend or gain *grit.*" replace="Improved quarry"}

::ab[Nemesis Slayer (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power flavor="A wild soul becomes death incarnate to his nemeses." standard="He can make a single attack against a *nemesis* at his full attack bonus. If the attack hits, the target takes damage normally and must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + half the wild soul's level + his Wisdom modifier) or die. The wild soul can instead deal an amount of nonlethal damage equal to the creature's current hit points, which the target can negate with a successful save at the same DC." usage="Five times per day, but cannot be used against the same creature more than once in a 24-hour period" replace="Master hunter"}

wild_stalker.name: Wild Stalker
description: ## Wild Stalker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/68›

Civilization grows stronger and more decadent with each passing year. It tears into unclaimed wilderness and destroys the fragile ecology in its constant push for expansion and exploitation. The wild stalker forsakes the bonds of community and lives in the trackless wilds far from others of his kind, or perhaps grew up there, never knowing of civilization as anything more than his enemy. He drives pioneers back to civilization and strives to keep the land unspoiled.

::ab[Strong Senses (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-boost flavor="A wild stalker's life among the wild has sharpened his senses." ability="He gains low-light vision and a bonus on Perception checks equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› of his ranger level. If he already has low-light vision, the bonus becomes 2 + one-fourth of his ranger level.@FN1" replace="1st favored enemy"}
::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def ability="A wild stalker gains ‹ability/uncanny dodge› as the barbarian class feature." replace="2nd-level combat style feat"}

::ab[Rage of the Wild (Ex)]{l=4 head icon=power ability="A wild stalker gains the *rage* ability as the ‹class/barbarian› class feature, but its barbarian level is considered to be his ranger level - 3." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Rage (Ex)]{next icon=boost-down flavor="Text from the barbarian class." usage="2 rounds/day + double your barbarian level + your Constitution modifier" free="You can enter (or end) a rage. You gain a +4 morale bonus to your Strength and Constitution, as well as a +2 morale bonus on Will saves. The increase to Constitution grants you 2 hit points per Hit Dice, but these disappear when the rage ends and are not lost first like ‹rule/temporary hit points›." order=ufp passive="While in rage, you take a -2 penalty to Armor Class, and you cannot use any Charisma-, Dexterity-, or Intelligence-based skills (except Acrobatics, Fly, Intimidate, and Ride) or any ability that requires patience or concentration. You are ‹misc/fatigued› after *rage* for a number of rounds equal to 2 times the number of rounds spent in the rage. You cannot enter a new *rage* while fatigued or ‹misc/exhausted› but can otherwise enter *rage* multiple times during a single encounter or combat. If you fall unconscious, your rage immediately ends, placing you in peril of death." special="Temporary increases to Constitution, such as those gained from rage and spells like ‹spell/bear's endurance›, do not increase the total number of rounds that a barbarian can rage per day. The total number of rounds of rage per day is renewed after resting for 8 hours, although these hours do not need to be consecutive."}

::ab[Rage Powers]{l=5 icon=power ability="A wild stalker ranger gains a single ‹ability/rage power<s›, as the barbarian class feature." repeat="He gains another rage power.~10~5" replace="2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 5th favored enemy"}

::ab[Wild Talents (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power-boost choice="A wild stalker can either take a rage power, or gain a +2 insight bonus into any one of the following skills: Acrobatics, Climb, Perception, Stealth, Survival, or Swim.@FN2" repeat="The wild stalker can gain one of these two benefits again.~10~4" replace="6th-, 10th-, 14th-, and 18th-level combat style feat"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[The original text reads: *"This bonus increases by +1 for every four levels after 1st."* This implies a 1/5/9/13/17 progression, which would only reach +5/+6 at 20th level, but the text continues, *"(to a maximum of +6 &#91&or +7&#93& at 20th level),"* which only makes sense if the bonus increases *at 4th level* and every four levels thereafter. This is the interpretation used here.]{from=1}

@FN[The original text reads: *"The wild stalker can gain one of these two benefits again every five levels after 6th (to a maximum of 4 times at 20th level)."* The listed progression only gives three benefits (at levels 6/11/16), not four. Either the maximum is wrong, or the level progression is wrong. Given that this ability replaces four bonus feats (at levels 6/10/14/18), the latter interpretation has been used here, and the ability is granted every *four* levels instead of five (which matches the levels of the bonus feats).]{from=2}

wilderness_explorer.name: Wilderness Explorer
description: ## Wilderness Explorer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/74›
Wilderness explorers are adept at making peaceful contact with remote societies.
::cskill{gain=diplo~kl lose=intm~kd}

::ab[Cultural Contact (Ex)]{icon=power order=pa passive="A wilderness explorer gains the ability to communicate with creatures with which he doesn't share a common language. Only basic concepts can be conveyed or understood, such as &quot&I need help,&quot& &quot&I come in peace,&quot& &quot&Come over here,&quot& &quot&Go over there,&quot& and the like. This allows the wilderness explorer to ignore the common language requirement of social interaction and language-dependent spells and effects, but he can do so only when the information being shared is relatively simple. The creature with which the wilderness explorer communicates must understand at least one language for this ability to function." ability="Additionally, a wilderness explorer can peacefully influence the initial attitude of humanoids that have Intelligence scores of 2 or lower. This ability functions just like *wild empathy,* but it works on humanoids instead of on animals. The wilderness explorer can also use this ability to influence a monstrous humanoid with 2 or lower Intelligence, but the wilderness explorer takes a -4 penalty on the check." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Hazard Sense (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def flavor="A wilderness explorer gains an intuitive understanding of natural hazards and traps fashioned from a natural environment." passive="He gains a bonus on Reflex saves against natural hazards and wilderness traps and a dodge bonus to AC against attacks from wilderness traps. Both of these bonuses are equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of his ranger level." replace="Hunter's bond"}

::ab[Indigenous Spirit (Su)]{l=8 icon=def flavor="A wilderness explorer attracts the aid of native spirits, which reward his appreciation of their home with whispered warnings." ability="The wilderness explorer gains ‹ability/uncanny dodge› as long as he remains within one of his chosen *favored terrains.* This works like the barbarian ability of the same name." replace="Swift tracker" alter="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Guardian Spirit (Su)]{l=11 icon=def flavor="A wilderness explorer gains the aid of indigenous spirits, which favor him when he is defending himself against multiple foes that surround him." ability="He gains ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge›; this ability functions only while the ranger is within one of his *favored terrains.*" replace=Quarry alter="Favored terrain"}

::ab[Manifest Spirit (Su)]{l=19 icon=def flavor="A wilderness explorer is shielded from magic by those guardian spirits that favor him." special="He gains spell resistance equal to 11 + his ranger level. This ability functions only while the wilderness explorer is within one of his *favored terrains.*" replace="Improved quarry" alter="Favored terrain"}

wilderness_medic.name: Wilderness Medic
description: ## Wilderness Medic
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/5›
Wilderness medics know a variety of ways they can supply and preserve a rebellion or similar movement without requiring any resources that might make them easier to track down.

::ab[Herbalist Training (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-boost ability="A wilderness medic can attempt Profession (herbalism) checks untrained and the wilderness medic gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his ranger level (minimum +1) on Heal checks and Profession (herbalism) checks. When he successfully ‹rule/forag«es<ing› for herbs, he finds twice as many doses of any herb he would normally." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Herbal Medicine (Ex)]{l=3 icon=aid ability="A wilderness medic can mimic the effects of ‹spell/stabilize› at will in any wilderness area by spending one use of a ‹eq-misc/healer's kit› as long as he can see the target." order=asu useMod=Wisdom3 standard="The wilderness medic can use encouraging words and herbs to lessen the severity of certain conditions affecting a touched target. If the target is ‹misc/exhausted›, it becomes ‹misc/fatigued› instead. If the target is ‹misc/frightened›, it becomes ‹misc/shaken› instead. If the target is ‹misc/nauseated›, it becomes ‹misc/sickened› instead. The duration of the effect is unchanged; the effect is simply reduced to the lesser version." imp9="The wilderness medic can lessen the severity of additional conditions for the target and cure other conditions that the target suffers. If the target creature is ‹misc/blinded›, it becomes ‹misc/dazzled› instead. If the target is ‹misc/panicked›, it becomes shaken instead. If it has any of the following conditions, they are cured: ‹misc/deafened›, fatigued, shaken, and sickened." special="The wilderness medic is able to use the non-*stabilize* ability on a given touched target once every 24 hours." replace=Endurance~evasion}

::ab[Rallying Bond (Sp)]{icon=down-aid-boost l4="A wilderness medic must choose his companions for his *hunter's bond.*" l9="The wilderness medic can encourage all of his companions when he uses his *hunter's bond* to benefit his companions (see the ranger's *hunter's bond* ability). As a @HLfree_action when the wilderness medic uses a @HLmove_action to grant affected allies half his *favored enemy* bonus, he can also grant affected allies the benefits of ‹spell/remove fear›." l14="When the wilderness medic uses this ability, he can instead affect all allies that benefit from his *hunter's bond* with ‹spell/blessing of fervor›, using his ranger level as his caster level." useMod=Wisdom alter="Hunter's bond" replace="Improved evasion"}

witchguard.name: Witchguard
description: ## Witchguard
‹SOURCE People of the North/25›

Witchguards are the sworn defenders of the White Witches of Irrisen. Although the White Witches are spellcasters of immense power, even they cannot defend themselves against every attack. Witchguards dedicate their lives to protecting their charges, and they gain some arcane knowledge in return. They are trained to work closely with witches and defend their charges from harm. Witchguards learn magical abilities from the same patrons that grant witches their powers, though they can use their abilities to protect any kind of spellcaster, not just a witch. A witchguard is ready to face any foe that might threaten his charge, and he is ever prepared to lay down his life to protect the life of the person he is sworn to defend.
::cskill{gain=kno~sm lose=ha~kd}

::ab[Defend Charge (Ex)]{l=4 icon=protect useInc=ranger~3~4 useF="4~1~3" ability="A witchguard forms a bond with a spellcaster he has sworn to defend. This bond allows the witchguard to spend a @HLmove_action to grant an adjacent spellcaster a +2 dodge bonus to AC and a +2 circumstance bonus on ‹rule/concentration› checks. The bonuses last for a number of rounds equal to the witchguard's Wisdom modifier (minimum 1)." increment="p!These bonuses~0~5~4~2" replace="Hunter's bond"}
::ab[Bodyguard (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A witchguard gains ‹feat/Bodyguard› as a bonus feat, even if he doesn't meet the prerequisite." replace=Endurance}

::ab[Patron (Su)]{flavor="A witchguard learns something of arcane magic from the witches he defends." icon=learn l4="When a witchguard gains the ability to cast spells, he must also select a ‹ability/patron<s›, as the witch class feature. This patron is usually the same as the patron of the witch he is sworn to protect, but the witchguard may choose any patron. The witchguard adds the first four spells from his patron's spell list to his ranger spell list. The witchguard cannot cast patron spells of a level he is unable to cast."}
::ab[Sworn Defender (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A witchguard gains ‹feat/In Harm's Way› as a bonus feat." replace="Woodland stride"}

yokai_hunter.name: Yokai Hunter
description: ## Yokai Hunter
‹SOURCE Dragon Empires Primer/23›

Yokai hunters possess a supernatural tether to the spirit worlds that shroud mortal existence. They use their powers to peer into the realm of the dead, and are adept at hunting those who dwell in the realm between the Material Plane and the Great Beyond, the supernatural beings Tians refer to as yokai. The yokai hunter is an archetype of the ranger class.

::ab[Favored Yokai (Su)]{flavor="A yokai hunter possesses a supernatural ability to sense beings connected to the spirit world, such as ‹family/kami›, ‹family/oni›, and ‹template/ghost«s›, and is formidable at fighting them." icon=boost-power l=1 order=pa passive="A yokai hunter gains a +2 bonus on weapon attacks and damage rolls against one of the following creature types (and subtypes, if applicable), which are considered yokai for the purposes of this archetype: aberration, dragon, fey, magical beast, outsider (native), and undead (incorporeal). (A GM may choose to alter this list for the purposes of her campaign.)" repeat="A yokai hunter may select another *favored yokai* from the list above, gaining a +2 bonus on weapon attacks and damage rolls against that yokai.~5~5" ability="In addition, whenever a yokai hunter sees a *favored yokai* that is disguised or has not made its otherworldly essence known, he gains an automatic check to identify the creature as such. To make this check, a yokai hunter rolls 1d20 and adds his yokai hunter level and Charisma modifier (DC 10 + 1/2 the yokai's Hit Dice + the yokai's Cha modifier). A successful check means that the yokai hunter recognizes the creature's ties to the spiritual world. Regardless of whether the check is successful, a yokai hunter gains the bonus on weapon attacks and damage rolls against *favored yokai.*" special="Ranger abilities that rely on a *favored enemy* instead rely on this ability." replace="Favored enemy"}

::ab[Yokai Sense (Su)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A yokai hunter can sense yokai he has identified with his *favored yokai* ability as though he had ‹umr/blindsense› out to a range of 60 feet." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Resist Yokai (Su)]{l=12 icon=def passive="A yokai hunter can attempt to shrug off the magical energies of a yokai. He gains a +4 insight bonus on saving throws to resist the spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of *favored yokai.*" order=ps standard="Once per day, a yokai hunter can shed an effect placed on him by a yokai as ‹spell/break enchantment›, using his yokai hunter level as his caster level." replace=Camouflage}
